- CORE response-ledger authority cutover slice, July 3: `response-dispatcher.mjs` no longer writes initial delegated or Directive-posted response rows as silent old-ledger authority after CORE succeeds. `recordCoreHostContinueRelease(...)` now puts the dispatcher response id into the CORE `hostContinueReleased` projection, `core-store-v2` exposes that id in `responseLedger`, and dispatcher compatibility rows are tagged with `authority: "compatibilityProjection"`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, and compact `coreProjection` refs. Focused proof in `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs` now proves duplicate dispatch can be detected from CORE projections even when `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` is empty, so host generation is not released a second time from old-ledger state. Adjacent proof passed for `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and syntax checks on `response-dispatcher.mjs` / `core-store-v2.mjs`. Remaining Wave 1/2 blocker is broader update-path demotion: host-native completion/reobserve updates still mutate old response rows as compatibility state, and the next slice should either project those updates from CORE or prove the old row is only a resumable mirror.
- CORE response update-path demotion slice, July 3: `response-dispatcher.mjs` now routes host-native completion, delayed reobserve closure, contradiction completion patching, and failed/unavailable host-native recovery patches through a CORE projection-aware compatibility updater instead of direct old `updateDirectiveResponse(...)` calls. The updater reads `coreStoreV2` response projections after `recordVisibleResponse(...)` or CORE recovery marking, preserves the old row only as a resumable compatibility mirror, and tags the row with `authority: "compatibilityProjection"`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, and compact `coreProjection` refs. Focused proof in `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs` now covers normal host-native completion, completion-record diagnostic failure, delayed reobserve completion, failed host-native recovery, and failed-then-retried host-native completion as CORE-tagged compatibility rows with raw assistant text excluded. Adjacent proof passed for `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, and syntax on `response-dispatcher.mjs`. Remaining direct response-ledger mutators are outside the dispatcher: `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` retry/fallback response patches, `message-reconciler.mjs` mutation response updates, and `runtime-app.mjs` host callback/correct-as-swipe style response updates.
- Response-ledger fail-closed mirror slice, July 3: the dispatcher compatibility helpers now require CORE response projection evidence, CORE diagnostic evidence, or compact CORE release/completion evidence before writing an old `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` mirror. The raw success fallback is removed, and the later production fallback cutover also removed the synthetic missing-projection fallback rows: missing CORE evidence now throws `DIRECTIVE_CORE_RESPONSE_PROJECTION_REQUIRED` before `recordDirectiveResponse(...)` or `updateDirectiveResponse(...)` can write `compatibilityProjectionUnavailable`, `directive.coreResponseProjectionUnavailable.v1`, or a missing-projection mirror. HostContinue release, host-native completion, host-native failure recovery, host-native continuity recovery, delayed reobserve closure, and Directive-posted visible responses now name the mirrored operation and cite available CORE transaction/response/diagnostic ids. `state-delta-gateway.mjs` preserves `compatibilityMirror`, and the skeleton guard now rejects reintroducing raw or synthetic missing-projection fallbacks. Focused proof passed: `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. Remaining response-ledger work is broader REPAIR mirror isolation and Correct-as-Swipe response lifecycle demotion.
- CORE response mutation demotion slice, July 3: `message-reconciler.mjs` now tags directive-response edit/delete/selected-swipe source mutation rows as CORE compatibility projections once REPAIR/CORE records the source-mutation recovery. The old response row still carries invalidation fields needed for resume, prompt sync, and live proof, but its authority is explicit: `authority: "compatibilityProjection"`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, and `coreProjection.kind: "directive.coreResponseMutationProjectionRef.v1"` with transaction/recovery/event refs. Focused proof in `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs` covers response-id-first delete after host id reuse, host-only newest raw response selection before stale CORE projections, and CORE-tagged response mutation rows. Adjacent proof passed for `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, and syntax on `message-reconciler.mjs`. Remaining direct response-ledger mutators are now concentrated in `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` retry/fallback response patches, `runtime-app.mjs` host callback/correct-as-swipe style response updates, and the selected-swipe candidate setup/test helper.
- REPAIR source-mutation replacement-text demotion slice, July 3: `message-reconciler.mjs` no longer stores raw edited replacement text on old ingress/response compatibility rows for player-source or Directive-response source mutations. REPAIR still receives the replacement text as write-only input so CORE can record source-mutation hash evidence, but the old mirror rows now clear `replacementText` and preserve only `replacementTextPresent`, `replacementTextHash`, and `replacementTextLength`. This shrinks save growth and prevents old `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger` / `responseLedger` mirrors from becoming raw replacement-text archives. Focused proof updated in `test-message-recovery.mjs` and `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs` to assert raw edited text is absent from old mirrors while hash/presence/length evidence remains; `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` now rejects `replacementText: compact(replacementText)` mirror writes and requires `compactReplacementTextRef(...)`. Adjacent proof passed for `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- State-delta replacement-text sanitizer slice, July 3: `state-delta-gateway.mjs` now centrally sanitizes raw `replacementText` on old ingress/response ledger writes and updates. `recordTurnIngress(...)`, `updateTurnIngress(...)`, `recordDirectiveResponse(...)`, and `updateDirectiveResponse(...)` convert any raw replacement text into `replacementText: null`, `replacementTextPresent`, `replacementTextHash`, and `replacementTextLength`, preserving existing hash refs when callers already provide them. This makes the old ledgers safer as compatibility mirrors even if a future runtime caller accidentally passes raw edit text outside `MessageReconciler`. Live mutation proof scripts now treat `replacementTextPresent` / `replacementTextHash` as replacement evidence instead of relying on raw `response.replacementText`. Focused proof passed: `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs`, `test-campaign-sidecar-scheduler.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, and `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`; the skeleton guard now rejects direct raw replacement-text recording in the gateway.
- CORE ingress mutation projection slice, July 3: player-source edit/delete/selected-swipe old ingress rows now match the response mutation demotion pattern. `MessageReconciler` tags updated ingress rows with `authority: "compatibilityProjection"`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, and compact `directive.coreIngressMutationProjectionRef.v1` metadata that names the CORE transaction/recovery case, ingress id, event type, source kind, and projected status. `state-delta-gateway.mjs` now records and preserves ingress `coreRecovery`, `coreProjection`, `compatibilityMirror`, `projectionSource`, and `authority` so later ingress record merges cannot erase CORE mirror provenance. Focused proof passed: `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; the skeleton guard now rejects player-source mutation mirrors that lack CORE ingress projection metadata.
- CORE ingress recovery/restart projection slice, July 3: `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` now tags two remaining production ingress mirror updates as CORE compatibility projections. Classifier/turn-processing failures that have CORE recovery evidence write old ingress `recoveryRequired` rows with compact `directive.coreIngressRecoveryProjectionRef.v1` metadata, and latest-source reobserve/restart marks the superseded old ingress row with compact `directive.coreIngressSourceRestartProjectionRef.v1` metadata after `coreTurnStore.supersedeLatestSourceTransaction(...)`. These rows still carry resume/proof fields such as `error`, `restartedByIngressId`, and `restartRepairDecision`, but authority is explicit and CORE-backed. Focused proof passed: `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, and `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`. Next Wave 1 slice should follow the explorer finding: remove snapshot/history restore authority from terminal replay, scene recalculation, and replacement/rerun base-state paths unless backed by CORE checkpoint refs.
- Terminal checkpoint-only replay cut, July 3: `campaign-end-condition-service.mjs` no longer promotes old `turnLedger.snapshotBefore` or `runtimeTracking.history` snapshots into new terminal replay CORE checkpoint artifacts during end-condition detection. Terminal replay can still use an existing compact CORE checkpoint ref and loader, but old retained snapshots now fail closed as missing checkpoint evidence instead of becoming fresh replay authority. Focused proof in `test-campaign-end-condition-service.mjs` now asserts a `writeTerminalCheckpoint` hook is not called for raw `snapshotBefore`, pending terminal interactions do not receive synthetic checkpoint refs, repair-denied/missing-repair/replay success fixtures provide explicit CORE checkpoint refs/loaders, and raw checkpoint state stays out of REPAIR evidence. Static guard in `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` rejects reintroducing `retainedTerminalSnapshotRecord(...)`, `turnLedger.snapshotBefore` checkpoint source authority, or `runtimeTracking.history.outcomeSnapshot` checkpoint source authority. Adjacent proof passed for `test-runtime-stage18-rerun-branch-recovery.mjs` and `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs`. Remaining checkpoint-only blocker: `scene-reconciliation.mjs` recalculation preview and `runtime-app.mjs` replacement commit base state still need CORE checkpoint loader/fail-closed cutover.
- Scene/rerun checkpoint-only cutover, July 3: `scene-reconciliation.mjs` now previews Recalculate From Here only from a loaded CORE checkpoint ref. Inline `turnLedger.snapshotBefore` no longer authorizes scratch replay; old snapshot-only entries produce a stopped preview without calling the checkpoint loader or replay director. Preview records now carry compact `coreCheckpointRef` and `snapshotSourceKind` evidence when available. `runtime-app.mjs` now requires a CORE checkpoint ref plus loaded snapshot before committing a pending outcome replacement, and `previewOutcomeReplacement(...)` stores the compact checkpoint ref/source kind on the pending replacement so commit cannot silently fall back to current campaign state or an inline old snapshot. Focused proof passed: `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs`, `test-runtime-stage18-rerun-branch-recovery.mjs`, `test-campaign-end-condition-service.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`. This closes the explorer-identified snapshot/history restore-authority blocker for terminal replay, scene recalculation preview, and outcome replacement commit; remaining Wave 1 work is broader runtime resume/replay semantics and old-ledger writer API containment.
- Terminal checkpoint metadata source cut, July 3: `detectCampaignEndCondition(...)` no longer reports `preOutcomeSnapshot` as retained checkpoint authority when a `turnLedger` entry still carries an old inline `snapshotBefore`. Pending terminal interactions now advertise `source: coreCheckpoint` only when a compact CORE checkpoint ref exists on the turn entry or last committed turn marker; otherwise they fall back to non-retained checkpoint policy metadata. Package end-condition fixtures now provide compact CORE refs, the UI contract renders `CoreCheckpoint`, and `test-end-condition-evaluator.mjs` proves a raw inline snapshot without a CORE ref becomes `lastStableAutosave`/`retained: false` with no synthetic checkpoint ref. Focused proof passed for `test-end-condition-evaluator.mjs`, all bundled package end-condition tests, `test-terminal-catastrophic-command.mjs`, `test-campaign-end-condition-service.mjs`, `test-end-condition-ui-contracts.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. Remaining Wave 1 work is unchanged: old-ledger writer API containment and storage materialization demotion.
- Old-ledger writer containment slice, July 3: `state-delta-gateway.mjs` now classifies authority centrally for `recordTurnIngress(...)` and `recordDirectiveResponse(...)`. Callers that supply explicit authority keep it; CORE-observed ingress rows without compatibility metadata become `authority: "coreIngressProjection"` with `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"` and compact mirror evidence; rows with CORE projection/recovery/release/completion evidence default to compatibility projection metadata; rows with no CORE evidence are marked `authority: "compatibilityProjectionUnavailable"` with `projectionSource: "runtimeTrackingLegacy"` and a compact mirror status of `missingCoreProjection`. This keeps bridge rows usable for resume/proof while preventing silent old-ledger authority by omission. `recordTurnIngress(...)` also preserves existing CORE authority metadata on later merge calls that only refresh host/text/status fields. Focused proof passed: `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs`, and `test-correct-as-swipe-workflow.mjs`. Remaining old-ledger work is hard removal or stricter write-mode enforcement once every production caller has CORE projection evidence at entry.
- Old-ledger update-writer authority normalization slice, July 3: `state-delta-gateway.mjs` now applies the same authority classification to `updateTurnIngress(...)` and `updateDirectiveResponse(...)` that append writers already used. Imported or previously silent old ingress/response rows touched by runtime code can no longer remain authority-free after mutation: status-only updates become explicit `compatibilityProjectionUnavailable` / `runtimeTrackingLegacy` rows with compact `missingCoreProjection` mirrors, while later updates carrying CORE projection evidence can upgrade stale missing-projection rows to `compatibilityProjection` / `coreStoreV2` without being pinned by their prior unavailable authority. This reduces save-growth and authority ambiguity from old imported rows that only become visible after edit/delete/retry/correction paths touch them. `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` proves silent imported ingress/response rows are tagged on update and that a CORE response projection patch upgrades the row to CORE compatibility authority; `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards both update writers and the patch-evidence upgrade helper. Focused/adjacent proof passed for syntax on `state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, plus `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; development live proof remains one ST soak user/lane until broad soak is re-enabled.
- CORE provider-failure retry projection slice, July 3: `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` now tags provider-failure-after-mechanics fallback response rows and subsequent assistant-swipe retry closure rows as CORE compatibility projections. The fallback still leaves CORE in `responseRetryRequired` until the selected assistant swipe retry closes the recovery, but the old response row now carries `authority: "compatibilityProjection"`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, and `coreProjection.kind: "directive.coreResponseRetryProjectionRef.v1"` with transaction/recovery/status refs for fallback, retry closure failure, and retry completion. Focused proof in `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` asserts fallback, synthetic CORE retry-closure failure, and successful selected retry all carry CORE projection metadata while not rerunning mechanics or recreating old recovery rows. Adjacent proof passed for `test-chat-response-recovery.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and syntax on `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`. Remaining direct response-ledger mutators are mostly `runtime-app.mjs` host callback/correct-as-swipe style updates plus selected-swipe live setup helpers; generic retry dispatch is already routed through `response-dispatcher` and inherits its CORE projection metadata.
- Runtime-app Outcome Integrity / Correct-as-Swipe response projection slice, July 3: `runtime-app.mjs` now tags Outcome Integrity review rejection, accepted prose-edit metadata, Correct-as-Swipe candidate append, and Correct-as-Swipe lifecycle response-row updates as CORE compatibility projections instead of silent old response-ledger authority. Rows still retain compact `outcomeIntegrity` / `correctAsSwipe` resume metadata, but their owner is explicit through `authority: "compatibilityProjection"`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, and compact `coreProjection.kind` values (`directive.coreResponseOutcomeIntegrityProjectionRef.v1` or `directive.coreResponseCorrectAsSwipeProjectionRef.v1`) with only ids, hashes, statuses, transaction refs, and review verdict refs. `recordDirectiveResponse(...)` now preserves `correctAsSwipe` so CORE-only Correct-as-Swipe fallback rows do not drop case metadata. The selected-swipe live proof setup helper now tags its opt-in setup response row as `directive.coreResponseSelectedSwipeSetupProjectionRef.v1` with hash-only setup evidence, so staged native-swipe proof no longer seeds an unowned old row. Focused proof in `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs` asserts app-level accepted Outcome Integrity edit rows carry CORE projection metadata without raw edited prose in the projection, `test-correct-as-swipe-workflow.mjs` asserts `recordDirectiveResponse(...)` preserves Correct-as-Swipe case/projection metadata, and `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs` asserts the setup helper remains CORE-tagged. Adjacent proof passed for `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, and syntax on `runtime-app.mjs`, `state-delta-gateway.mjs`, and `run-sillytavern-selected-swipe-actuation-live.mjs`. Remaining direct response-ledger mutator debt is now mostly test-only setup or broad grep cleanup; production paths in dispatcher, reconciler, orchestrator, and runtime-app are tagged compatibility projections at focused-test depth.
- Runtime-app Correct-as-Swipe raw response fallback removal slice, July 3: `runtime-app.mjs` no longer lets `proposeCorrectAsSwipeCandidate(...)` decide candidate append record/update behavior by scanning raw `tracked.runtimeTracking.responseLedger`. The action now starts from `createRuntimeLedgerView(tracked).responseLedger`, infers whether an actual compatibility row exists from explicit projection metadata (`authority`, `compatibilityMirror`, or `projectionSource`), and otherwise records a fresh compact CORE compatibility projection row from the CORE/view response. This keeps Correct-as-Swipe from reviving or mutating silent raw old rows while preserving CORE-only projection support and tagged mirror update behavior. A single explorer agent audited remaining `runtime-app` raw-ledger seams and identified this as the smallest production authority leak before broader FORGE/source-current demotion. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` now rejects the removed `hotResponse` fallback and guards the projection-metadata branch; `test-correct-as-swipe-workflow.mjs` covers compact Correct-as-Swipe projection metadata. No live soak lanes were run.
- Runtime ledger read-path demotion slice, July 3: `runtime-ledger-view.mjs` now suppresses silent unmatched old ingress/response rows whenever any CORE projection is available. Unmatched old rows can still appear only when they are explicitly tagged compatibility projection mirrors (`authority` plus `compatibilityMirror` or `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`), so old ledgers remain status mirrors rather than hidden durable authority. Recovery reads are stricter: any CORE projection availability disables old `runtimeTracking.recoveryJournal` fallback, not only when CORE recovery rows exist. Focused proof in `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs` asserts CORE rows replace matching old rows, tagged compatibility mirrors survive, silent old-only rows are not findable by host id, duplicate SillyTavern host ids still resolve to tagged current projections, and recovery remains CORE-first. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` now guards the tagged fallback helper and recovery suppression shape. This closes another old-ledger read/projection demotion gap without running live soak lanes.
- MessageReconciler source-lookup demotion slice, July 3: `message-reconciler.mjs` no longer performs raw `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger` / `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` lookup before `runtime-ledger-view` when handling edit/delete/selected-swipe source mutations. Explicit `ingressId` / `responseId` and host-message-id matching now go through the same CORE-first ledger view as visibility changes, so silent old rows cannot bypass CORE projection demotion after a CORE projection exists. Tagged compatibility mirrors still work for stable SillyTavern `mesid` reindex cases, because the ledger view keeps those mirrors while suppressing untagged old rows. Focused proof in `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs` now covers stable-id response delete through a tagged current projection, host-id delete with stale CORE projection plus tagged current mirror, and explicit response-id delete against an untagged old row being ignored without CORE recovery mutation. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against raw-first lookup returning. This moves source-mutation recovery closer to pure CORE/REPAIR projection ownership without broad live soak.
- ResponseDispatcher compatibility-update demotion slice, July 3: `response-dispatcher.mjs` no longer uses a raw `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` helper when updating existing response compatibility rows. `updateDirectiveResponseCompatibility(...)` now merges existing metadata through `findResponse(...)`, which uses `runtime-ledger-view` and therefore inherits CORE-first projection demotion. The slice also fixed a ledger-view merge bug exposed by this cutover: composite response keys such as `turnId/outcomeId/responseKind` now require a complete tuple, so unrelated host-generation rows cannot merge merely because they share `responseKind`. Status arbitration now preserves failure/recovery patch statuses such as `unavailable` and `failed` instead of letting stale CORE response projection status downgrade them to `posted`. - Missing-CORE compatibility mirror quarantine, July 3: `runtime-ledger-view.mjs` now treats `authority: "compatibilityProjectionUnavailable"` rows as quarantine telemetry rather than fallback runtime authority whenever CORE projections exist. `state-delta-gateway.mjs` may still tag missing-CORE old ingress/response writes so callers and diagnostics can see the gap, but CORE-backed runtime reads, state commit carry-forward, generic restore, and REPAIR rollback restore suppress those rows instead of carrying them as resumable mirrors. `runtime-app.mjs` still uses missing-CORE rows as blockers for declaring complete CORE runtime authority, which is intentionally different from using them as read authority. - Active-save loaded CORE read-projection filter slice, July 3: `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` now filters loaded ingress and response runtime bridge rows before exposing them as `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections`. Previously loaded recovery projections were filtered to explicit `compatibilityProjection` / `coreStoreV2` bridge rows, but loaded ingress/response arrays were copied wholesale from rehydrated `runtimeTracking`; that could promote `runtimeBridgeV2` or missing-core compatibility mirrors into CORE read projections after load. Loaded ingress projections now keep only explicit CORE ingress bridge rows (`compatibilityProjection` or `coreIngressProjection`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, `directive.coreIngressCompatibilityMirror.v1`), and loaded response projections now keep only explicit CORE response bridge rows (`compatibilityProjection`, `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"`, `directive.coreResponseCompatibilityMirror.v1`). Runtime tracking can still retain tagged missing-core mirrors for compatibility, but nested CORE read projections no longer treat them as CORE authority. `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs` proves partial CORE load preserves tagged legacy runtime rows while omitting those rows from loaded CORE read projections, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the loaded ingress/response filters plus no raw `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger` / `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` copy into read projections. - REPAIR rollback restore demotion slice, July 3: `repair-command-boundary.mjs` no longer copies raw current old ingress, response, and recovery ledgers wholesale when applying a CORE checkpoint rollback. It now restores domain state from the checkpoint, then grafts runtime ledger state from `createRuntimeLedgerViewAsync(...)`, using embedded `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections` or the external `coreTurnStore` when present. That keeps CORE rows and tagged compatibility mirrors, suppresses silent old ingress/response rows once CORE projections exist, and keeps recovery rows CORE-only under CORE projection availability. A single explorer agent audited the seam and identified the external-store hydration caveat; the implementation closes that caveat instead of relying only on loaded save evidence. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` now covers embedded CORE projection demotion, external `coreTurnStore` projection demotion, and no raw ledger-array copyback. Pure-v2 loads attach transient `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections` with `runtimeAuthority: "coreStoreV2"` plus ingress, response, and turn projections, while the compatibility-shaped runtime rows are tagged `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"` / `authority: "compatibilityProjection"` so shared runtime reads have an owner source. `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs` now proves hot-appended pure-v2 ingress/response/turn rows appear in CORE read projections on load and active recovery; `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the repository projection builder. - V2 storage load projection demotion slice, July 3: `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` and `directive-storage-repository.mjs` no longer hydrate CORE event projections back into authoritative old `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger`, `runtimeTracking.responseLedger`, or `runtimeTracking.recoveryJournal` rows on v2 load. Loaded CORE rows live in transient `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections` with `runtimeAuthority: "coreStoreV2"`; old runtime ledgers are compact resume scaffolding unless current runtime work writes explicit hot compatibility mirrors. Missing-CORE rows tagged `compatibilityProjectionUnavailable` no longer serialize/reload as CORE store projections. To keep active turns functional after load, `runtime-ledger-view.mjs` now has an explicit `runtimeOverlay: true` mode for current-runtime contexts, and `runtime-app.mjs`, `state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `campaign-sidecar-scheduler.mjs`, and `turn-commit-coordinator.mjs` use that hot overlay only where in-memory rows must join loaded CORE projections for source guards, sidecar diagnostics, mechanics commit lookup, and state commits. This resolved two post-load strict-view leaks: regular sidecar CORE diagnostics were dropped when the scheduler could not see the hot ingress transaction, and reloaded turn mechanics skipped CORE commit when the turn coordinator could not recover the hot ingress transaction. - Direct old-ledger writer debt closure, July 3: a focused explorer audit found four remaining production ingress/response writer seams that could still repopulate old runtime ledgers without clear CORE authority: terminal outcome fallback ingress recording, response-post-failure ingress retry tagging, host-native continuity compatibility ingress patching, and Correct-as-Swipe selected response updates. `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `response-dispatcher.mjs`, and `message-reconciler.mjs` now either attach compact CORE projection evidence with `authority: "compatibilityProjection"` / `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"` or explicitly quarantine missing-CORE fallback rows. The audit also confirmed the gateway choke point, dispatcher response writers, runtime-app outcome rerun ingress path, and storage projections were already on the intended authority model. Durable sidecar/background evidence remains owned by CORE diagnostics, active-save projections, and `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.sidecarDiagnostics` / `backgroundBatches`; old sidecar rows are no longer preserved as hot save baggage. - Runtime-app response projection merge demotion, July 3: `mergeFresherResponseLedgerProjection(...)` no longer resurrects fresher in-memory response rows by writing them into old `runtimeTracking.responseLedger`. The merge now reads only `readRuntimeCoreProjections(inMemoryState).responseLedger`, writes fresher rows into transient `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.responseLedger`, and matches only stable CORE ids, response ids, transaction ids, or turn/outcome/response-kind tuples. It no longer accepts tagged old rows without CORE projection evidence, and it no longer matches by SillyTavern `hostMessageId`, avoiding the positional-host-id collision class seen during edit/delete/reobserve work. - Active-save ingress/response projection cutover, July 3: `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` now persists active-save ingress and response projections only from `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.ingressLedger` / `responseLedger`. Old `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger` and `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` rows no longer enter materialized heads, host maps, event segments, or loaded CORE read projections even when the old rows are tagged `compatibilityProjection` / `coreStoreV2` but lack a matching CORE projection row. Load-time response coalescing also no longer uses SillyTavern `hostMessageId` as a merge key, so a missing-CORE legacy event row sharing only a positional host id cannot overwrite or erase a valid CORE response projection before filtering. The repository runtime-bridge no-transaction fixture now expects no event segment rather than a no-CORE runtimeBridge event segment. - Active-save recovery bridge authority slice, July 3: `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` now materializes recovery rows loaded from v2 event segments with the same explicit compatibility authority metadata already used for ingress/response rows. `runtimeRecoveryLedgerFromEvents(...)` attaches `authority`, `projectionSource`, and a compact `directive.coreRecoveryCompatibilityMirror.v1` ref, so reloaded recovery projections cannot look like silent old `runtimeTracking.recoveryJournal` authority when the active save is re-persisted or inspected. `coreStoreReadProjectionsFromLoadedArtifacts(...)` now filters loaded recovery rows through `loadedCoreRecoveryProjectionRows(...)`, so only explicit `compatibilityProjection` / `coreStoreV2` recovery mirrors become CORE read projections; runtimeBridge-only recovery rows stay tagged telemetry and do not become authority. Initial persist remains CORE-only through `projectedRecoveryRows(...)`; this slice closes the reload/materialization side of the bridge. `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs` proves loaded recovery rows carry `compatibilityProjection`, `coreStoreV2`, and the recovery mirror kind while still suppressing old `recovery-1`, and also proves runtimeBridge-only recovery rows are tagged `compatibilityProjectionUnavailable` but omitted from CORE read projections. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards recovery bridge authority and rejects raw `runtimeTracking.recoveryJournal` copying into CORE read projections. Focused/adjacent proof passed for syntax on `active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, plus `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, and `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Scene Handshake recovery-safety demotion slice, July 3: `scene-handshake-settler.mjs` now builds snapshot `safety.pendingRecoveryCount` from `createRuntimeLedgerView(state).recoveryJournal` instead of raw `state.runtimeTracking.recoveryJournal`. Stale legacy recovery rows therefore cannot inflate Scene Handshake safety context, trigger review pressure, or keep old recovery baggage visible in Utility-lane prompt snapshots once CORE recovery projections exist or the initializer has filtered old rows. `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs` proves a raw legacy recovery row yields `pendingRecoveryCount: 0` in the generated prompt while explicit CORE recovery projection evidence yields a count of `1`. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the shared ledger-view import and rejects direct raw recovery counting. Focused proof passed for syntax on `scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, plus `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs` and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Scene Reconciliation source-lookup demotion slice, July 3: `scene-reconciliation.mjs` now resolves message anchors, range outcome ids, and SRE transaction ids through `createRuntimeLedgerView(state)` instead of raw `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger` / `runtimeTracking.responseLedger`. Legacy-only saves still work through the shared fallback, but once CORE projections exist, stale raw rows cannot drive reconciliation anchors, recalculation outcome selection, or source-settlement transactions. `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs` now proves an explicit CORE ingress projection supplies the SRE transaction while conflicting raw legacy ingress/response rows are ignored. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the shared ledger-view import and rejects raw ingress/response reads in Scene Reconciliation source authority. Focused proof passed for syntax on `scene-reconciliation.mjs`, `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, plus `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs` and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Production old-ledger quarantine writer cutover, July 3: the remaining production `missingCoreWriteMode: "quarantine"` writers in the hot chat-turn and response-dispatch paths are gone. Generic chat-turn ingress updates now require existing or patch-supplied CORE projection evidence and throw `DIRECTIVE_CORE_INGRESS_UPDATE_REQUIRED` before touching the old ingress ledger when evidence is absent; host-response post-failure ingress updates and ResponseDispatcher host-native continuity ingress patches now always use `missingCoreWriteMode: "reject"` instead of conditionally quarantining missing-CORE writes. This makes old-ledger quarantine a gateway/test-only compatibility concept rather than a production escape hatch in current turn dispatch, recovery, and host-native continuity projection. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` now guards reject-only production behavior for those seams. Focused proof passed for syntax on `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `response-dispatcher.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, plus `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, and `test-chat-turn-terminal-outcome.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; current live-testing cadence remains one non-human ST user/lane only unless broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Runtime-app outcome-rerun ingress projection slice, July 3: committed outcome rerun now tags its replacement ingress as a CORE compatibility projection before the old ingress mirror is written. `beginOutcomeRerunReplacementTransaction(...)` builds `directive.coreIngressOutcomeRerunProjectionRef.v1` with replacement/replaced transaction ids, replacement ingress id, replaced/replacement outcome ids, replacement turn id, and source frame id; `commitProvisionalDirectorTurn(...)` records the replacement ingress only with `missingCoreWriteMode: "reject"`. This removes another direct old-ledger writer ambiguity in the rerun path while preserving the existing CORE mechanics checkpoint and outcome replacement proof. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards both the projection metadata and reject-only runtime-app ingress writer. Focused proof passed for syntax on `runtime-app.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, plus `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-runtime-stage18-rerun-branch-recovery.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- ResponseDispatcher missing-response-projection fail-closed cutover, July 3: `recordDirectiveResponseCompatibility(...)` and `updateDirectiveResponseCompatibility(...)` no longer synthesize `compatibilityProjectionUnavailable` response rows when CORE response projection, CORE diagnostic, CORE release/completion, or CORE recovery evidence are absent. The old `missingCoreResponseProjectionPatch(...)` / `directive.coreResponseProjectionUnavailable.v1` fallback is gone; missing evidence now throws `DIRECTIVE_CORE_RESPONSE_PROJECTION_REQUIRED` before old response-ledger mutation. Provider-failure-after-mechanics retry rows remain valid because `readCoreResponseProjectionFor(...)` accepts compact CORE recovery projection evidence, while the no-diagnostic visible-response failure fixture proves host posting and failed CORE/diagnostic attempts do not create old response rows, old recovery rows, or ingress recovery patches. Focused proof passed for syntax on `response-dispatcher.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, plus `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs` and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Pending-interaction resolution response authority slice, July 3: `handlePendingInteractionResolution(...)` now carries the real player resolution ingress through `resolveInteraction(...)`, restores that CORE-backed ingress after the provisional commit overwrites runtime state, and dispatches the visible committed response against the real resolution ingress rather than the synthetic audit-only `interaction:...:resolution:...` id. The synthetic resolution id remains on post-commit conversation and terminal checkpoint audit payloads, but response projection authority comes from the actual player confirmation message. This closes the risk-confirmation path that otherwise failed after response-dispatcher missing-projection fallback removal. Focused proof passed in `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` for risk pause/confirm and provider-failure fallback, and in `test-chat-turn-terminal-outcome.mjs` for terminal checkpoint posting/resolution. No live soak lanes were run.
- Direct ingress update fail-closed hardening, July 3: a one-agent review found that several remaining `updateTurnIngress(...)` mirrors were relying on gateway defaults or existing-row evidence rather than carrying explicit reject policy at the callsite. `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` and `message-reconciler.mjs` now pass `missingCoreWriteMode: "reject"` on every remaining direct ingress compatibility update in the hot pending-interaction, retry-completion, provider-failure, source-restart, turn-processing-failure, and source-mutation paths. Provider-failure-after-mechanics ingress retry now carries `directive.coreIngressResponseRetryProjectionRef.v1` evidence, and `updateIngressState(...)` no longer accepts any `compatibilityMirror` as existing evidence: only `authority: "compatibilityProjection"` plus `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"` qualifies, so `compatibilityProjectionUnavailable` rows cannot authorize further old-ledger mutation. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` now guards reject-only direct ingress updates, qualified CORE mirror evidence, and provider-failure ingress retry projection evidence. Focused proof passed for syntax on `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `message-reconciler.mjs`, and the skeleton test, plus `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-terminal-outcome.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, and `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; single-user live validation remains the current implementation policy.
- Witness-scoped fact source-frame gate, July 3: `buildContinuityFactIndex(...)` now accepts a `sourceFrame` and applies `isFactAllowedForSourceFrame(...)` to explicit witness-scoped facts before they can enter projection matrices, director packets, or player-safe continuity planning. The gate deliberately distinguishes explicit knowledge scope from the default `subjectIds` derived from `subject`, so generic Director-only mission facts remain available to Director audiences while private, false-belief, inferred, and even default-public facts with explicit `knownBy` / `witnessedBy` scope require a present/relevant actor or group from the current Frame. `buildContinuityProjectionMatrix(...)`, `buildContinuityDirectorPacket(...)`, and `buildPlayerSafePromptContextWithContinuityPlanner(...)` now pass their already-built source Frames into the fact index. `test-continuity-projection-foundation.mjs` proves Bronn sees Bronn/senior-staff private facts but not Sam-private or Sam public-but-scoped facts; `test-continuity-director-packets.mjs` proves crewDirector packets exclude Sam-private witness facts in a Bronn scene while preserving generic Director-only mission facts; and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the source-frame wiring. Focused proof passed for syntax on `fact-schema.mjs`, `fact-index.mjs`, `projection-matrix.mjs`, `director-packets.mjs`, `player-safe-prompt-context-builder.mjs`, and updated tests, plus `test-continuity-director-packets.mjs`, `test-continuity-projection-foundation.mjs`, `test-continuity-projection-plan-validator.mjs`, `test-continuity-projection-diagnostics.mjs`, `test-lens-prompt-budget-lane-contracts.mjs`, `test-player-safe-prompt-context.mjs`, `test-runtime-director-turn.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Alpha-gate integration proof after response-ledger demotion, July 3: after the dispatcher, reconciler, orchestrator, runtime-app, selected-swipe setup, state gateway, live readiness summary, stable-id mutation, and CORE response projection slices, `node tools\scripts\run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed all `201` checks. The run included `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs` through the mutation/live-runner gates, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, both 5000-message scale gates, the external-context readiness and summary artifact gates, release-bundle preflight, and repository structure verification. This is deterministic integration evidence only: it does not replace Stage 13 five-user live Ashes certification, but it confirms the current CORE projection demotion does not break the alpha gate.
- Bounded five-user Ashes live proof, July 2: `artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/2026-07-02T22-14-31-788Z/report.json` now returns `ok: true` / aggregate `warning` after `--resume`. The run used only `directive-soak-a` through `directive-soak-e`, left `default-user` untouched, and passed non-human lane selection, external-context readiness proof, host-native completion turn coverage, continuity prompt/source proof, external-context generation proof, persisted CORE generation-start timing, persisted CORE host-native completion, durable model-call failure policy, deterministic and model-assisted factual grounding, and model-assisted story-quality review. The initial pass found a real `hostContinueReleased` without visible assistant completion in `ashes-sidecars-timekeeping`; the resumed pass reused the four good lanes and reran that lane successfully. Follow-up full-certification preflight now recomputes fixture depth from the raw readiness probe rather than trusting stale aggregate fixture-depth summaries, and `--coverage-standard all-lanes` passes after accepting Memory Books active-book metadata plus valid range diagnostics as rich evidence even when a lane has no `1_memory` prompt key. The same strict preflight now treats aggregate/lane execution warnings from the bounded run as depth-only, so aggregate/lane execution pass and the remaining red checks are the real full-certification gaps: bounded three-turn depth, lower-than-full prompt/fact artifact depth, and lower-than-full external-context generation depth. SRE latest-pair model timeouts are capped at 8 seconds and classified as fallback-handled only for the no-mutation/no-prompt-authority `sourceSettlementLatestPair` role; this preserves fail-closed safety while removing the earlier 30-second latency tax from the release gate.
- Bounded five-user Ashes live proof refresh, July 3: the Stage 13 live coordinator now defaults to sequential live lane execution (`--lane-concurrency 1`, overrideable by `--lane-concurrency N` or `DIRECTIVE_CPM_FIVE_USER_LANE_CONCURRENCY`) after `artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/2026-07-03T04-34-11-378Z/report.json` showed a five-parallel-lane host/provider starvation pattern. The failing `ashes-mutation-reconciliation` lane released `hostContinue` from CORE quickly but never received a terminal host assistant row before the 300s smoke wait. Focused reruns for `ashes-mutation-reconciliation` (`2026-07-03T04-58-08-590Z`) and `ashes-drawer-projection` (`2026-07-03T05-54-32-969Z`) passed as single-lane bounded proof, confirming the architecture can reach CORE generation-start and host-native completion in isolation while the multi-lane host remains unreliable.
- Live smoke wait hardening, July 3: `smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs` now treats a successful direct `observeHostPlayerMessage(...)` result as observation progress instead of polling runtime-view counters for 300s after the same call already returned `handled: true`. This moves failures from opaque `Timed out waiting for browser condition after 300000ms` reports into the later CORE host-native completion proof, where the artifact records whether the required `soak-turn-03` row reached `visibleResponsePostedAt` or failed as `hostNativeAssistantUnavailable`. Focused proof: `test-live-soak-prep.mjs` and `test-continuity-matrix-five-user-soak-coordinator.mjs`.
- Current live blockers, July 3: the sequential bounded five-user run `artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/2026-07-03T06-06-18-497Z/report.json` still failed and should not be treated as certification evidence. Passing aggregate checks prove the main architecture timing work: five non-human users, readiness, external-context readiness, fixture depth, continuity prompt/source proof, generation-time external context proof, persisted CORE generation-start timing, and model-call failure policy all passed. Remaining blockers are now concrete: two lanes (`ashes-drawer-projection`, `ashes-command-bearing-endings`) recorded required `soak-turn-03` host-native completion failure even though other host continuations completed; `ashes-sidecars-timekeeping` produced a model-assisted factual-grounding failure with unparseable/truncated provider JSON that also flagged a real factual drift candidate (Breckenridge described at warp during the opening shuttle handoff, against the impulse-transfer canary); and `ashes-mutation-reconciliation` hit a `storyQualityReviewer` generation timeout. Next Stage 13 work should fix or mitigate these host/provider/content blockers rather than reworking CORE generation-start timing, which is already passing.
- Stage 13 blocker mitigation slice, July 3: host-native completion proof now classifies the exact targeted transaction/player host ids through `hostNativeCompletionTargetOutcome(...)`, waits for a target pass instead of returning early on a target failure while the proof window is still open, records target completion/failure counts in timeout artifacts, and raises the default host-native completion proof window from 240s to 360s. This addresses the observed 174-209s successful host-native latencies plus missing `soak-turn-03` rows without weakening release certification, because failed target rows still fail after the full wait and count-only proof still does not pass. The Ashes opening travel source now foregrounds the impulse transfer waypoint and explicitly bans warp-course/streaking-star visuals during the shuttle approach, landing, disembarkation, and immediate handoff scene; the travel fact materializer puts the impulse handoff before the background warp-flight-plan context; and factual canaries now include deterministic watch phrases for holding/streaking at warp during the opening handoff. The review-only smoke path now retries `storyQualityReviewer` once on timeout/empty output and records retry attempts, reducing one-shot provider flake without accepting stale, unparseable, or missing review evidence. Focused proof passed: `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-continuity-matrix-five-user-soak-coordinator.mjs`, `test-continuity-projection-foundation.mjs`, `test-factual-grounding-matrix-prompt-proof.mjs`, and syntax checks for `smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs`, `sillytavern-core-proof-policy.mjs`, and `factual-grounding-canaries.mjs`. Next proof step is a bounded live rerun of the affected lanes, then a fresh bounded five-user run before any 25-turn certification attempt.
- Temporary live-testing cadence, July 3: until the user explicitly re-enables broad soak, do not run all five live soak lanes for development proof. Use one non-human SillyTavern soak user/lane for targeted validation, keep `default-user` reserved for human testing, and treat five-lane/full-depth Ashes proof as final certification only. Latest one-lane check after the mitigation slice: `artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/2026-07-03T07-18-30-583Z/report.json` ran `ashes-sidecars-timekeeping` on `directive-soak-c` with `--turn-limit 3`, passed CORE generation-start timing, CORE host-native completion, deterministic and model-assisted factual grounding, external-context generation proof, and model-call failure policy. The story-quality provider path is no longer the blocker in this artifact: `storyQualityReviewer` returned `ok` in 7.2s on attempt 1 of 2. The remaining warning is real low-severity story-quality evidence, not timeout/harness failure: the model-assisted reviewer scored assistant message 5 at severity `low` for slight player-agency/POV drift around the phrase "someone who knew which precise question to ask." Treat follow-up as prompt/story-quality tuning, not provider retry work.
- Routine single-user soak policy code cut, July 3: `soak-sillytavern-campaign-live.mjs` now exports `SOAK_ROUTINE_SINGLE_USER_POLICY` and `selectRoutineSoakUser(...)`, so normal live execution has an explicit one-non-human-user selection contract instead of relying only on operator memory. The selector prefers `DIRECTIVE_SILLYTAVERN_USER`, otherwise selects the first non-human configured soak account, rejects `default-user`, and records ignored configured handles so broad `DIRECTIVE_SOAK_ST_USERS` values cannot be mistaken for routine fan-out. The existing `SOAK_PARALLEL_WORKER_POLICY` remains five-lane by design for final certification and explicit re-enable only. Focused proof in `test-live-soak-prep.mjs` covers multi-user configured input selecting one lane, explicit single-lane override, reserved human-user rejection, and preservation of the five-lane certification policy. No live soak lanes were run.
- Story-quality replay preflight policy cut, July 3: `replay-story-quality-review-preflight.mjs` now defaults each review-only replay to a 300000 ms child-process timeout, records `timeoutMs`, `maxLatencyMs`, and `retryCount` in the aggregate report, and passes `DIRECTIVE_SILLYTAVERN_STORY_QUALITY_REVIEW_RETRY_COUNT=1` into the review-only smoke by default. This makes provider flake mitigation explicit without treating stale, missing, timeout, or unparseable review evidence as a pass. Focused proof passed for `test-story-quality-review-preflight.mjs`, syntax checks for the preflight/test scripts, and `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Model-call diagnostic projection demotion, July 3: legacy save import now prefers `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.modelCallDiagnostics` over old `runtimeTracking.modelCallJournal` when summarizing and writing v2 diagnostics, matching the earlier sidecar/background demotion. Active-save v2 runtime summaries, resume cursors, and diagnostic segments now also prefer projected CORE model-call diagnostics over stale old `modelCallJournal` telemetry when both exist, while retaining compact old-journal fallback for old fixtures that have no CORE diagnostics. Active-save v2 load exposes compact model-call diagnostics in CORE read projections when runtime CORE evidence already exists, but model-call diagnostics alone no longer assert `runtimeAuthority: "coreStoreV2"` or suppress old ingress/response rows. This avoids a false-authority bug caught by `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, where diagnostic-only projections zeroed live ingress/response counts. Chat-native runtime view/freshness counters and factual/story review-only provider result paths now read compact CORE model-call diagnostics when attached, falling back to compact old telemetry only when projections are unavailable; the hot view keeps model-call count/list consistency and redaction while the cursor remains `runtimeResume.modelCallEventSequence`. Focused proof passed for `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-storage-cross-writer-v2.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-runtime-model-call-journal.mjs`, `test-runtime-host-injection.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Active-save response revision demotion, July 3: active-save v2 runtime summaries and resume cursors no longer use old `runtimeTracking.responseLedgerRevision` as a fallback when `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.responseLedgerRevision` is absent. This prevents stale old response-ledger counters from presenting as CORE resume authority after hot saves or materialized-head reloads. Focused proof in `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs` sets an old revision canary (`1234`) with no CORE revision and verifies both materialized-head `runtimeResume.responseLedgerRevision` and `runtimeSummary.responseLedgerRevision` stay `0`; the main fixture still proves an explicit CORE revision (`9`) wins over old runtimeTracking. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against reintroducing the `projection?.responseLedgerRevision ?? runtimeTracking.responseLedgerRevision` fallback. No live soak lanes were run.
- Runtime-app response revision demotion, July 3: runtime-app freshness counters, chat-native tracking, LENS prompt dirty-domain version vectors, and fresher in-memory response projection merge now treat `responseLedgerRevision` as CORE projection evidence only. Old `runtimeTracking.responseLedgerRevision` no longer makes an in-memory state look fresher, no longer appears as chat-native tracking freshness, and no longer gets promoted during response projection merge. When a CORE read projection supplies `responseLedgerRevision`, the merge carries it inside `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections` beside the merged response rows. Focused proof in `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs` verifies old revision canaries are ignored, explicit CORE revision wins, silent/tagged old merge candidates do not bump `runtimeTracking`, and CORE projection revision survives as projection evidence; `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the helper bodies. Adjacent proof passed for `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-runtime-host-injection.mjs`, and `test-runtime-model-call-journal.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Generic restore/rollback response revision demotion, July 3: generic state commits, generic state revision restore, and REPAIR rollback restore now derive `runtimeTracking.responseLedgerRevision` only from CORE read projection `responseLedgerRevision`. Stale old counters no longer survive commit/restore or rollback handoff; no CORE revision means the compatibility mirror field resets to `0`. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` covers CORE revision preservation (`77`) over stale old revision (`999`) and no-CORE reset over stale old revision (`66`) for both commit and generic restore. REPAIR rollback proof in `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` covers embedded CORE revision (`31`) and external CORE-store revision (`41`) winning over stale old rollback counters. Static guards now reject old `current.runtimeTracking.responseLedgerRevision` carry-forward in both helpers. Adjacent proof passed for `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Directive response update revision demotion, July 3: `updateDirectiveResponse(...)` no longer increments or preserves old `runtimeTracking.responseLedgerRevision` when it mutates a compatibility response row. The function now mirrors only the CORE read-projection `responseLedgerRevision`; without a CORE projection revision, the old mirror resets to `0`. This removes the last active old response-revision writer found in `state-delta-gateway.mjs` while leaving response rows as bounded compatibility projections. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` verifies an imported old row with stale revision `88` updates without preserving/incrementing the revision, a row-level CORE projection cannot synthesize a revision by itself, and explicit CORE read-projection revision `22` is carried instead of stale old revision `2200`. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the response-update helper against `tracking.responseLedgerRevision` and `responseLedgerRevision: updated` regression. Adjacent proof passed for `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, and `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Sidecar freshness count demotion, July 3: runtime-app freshness counters, chat-native tracking, LENS version vectors, and active-save v2 runtime summary/resume cursors no longer count old `runtimeTracking.sidecarJournal` rows as sidecar freshness. Sidecar freshness now comes from compact `runtimeResume.sidecarCount`, CORE `sidecarDiagnostics`, and accepted CORE `backgroundBatches` worker counts. Focused proof in `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs` verifies old sidecar rows alone count as `0` while CORE/background/runtimeResume evidence wins over larger old sidecar arrays. `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs` verifies materialized heads and runtime summaries ignore five old sidecar rows when CORE sidecar evidence is absent. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards both runtime-app and active-save helpers against reintroducing old sidecar-count authority. Adjacent proof passed for `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs` and `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Sidecar flush/proof count demotion, July 3: `flushChatSidecars()` and live smoke/harness snapshots no longer use old `runtimeTracking.sidecarJournal` length as primary sidecar progress when CORE sidecar/background projections are missing or lower. The old journal count remains visible only as explicit `sidecarJournalCount*` / `legacySidecarCount` telemetry; `sidecarCountBefore`, `sidecarCountAfter`, `sidecarDelta`, runtime snapshots, and sidecar activity classifiers now use CORE resume/diagnostic/background evidence plus concrete flush result count. Focused proof in `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs` pins `sidecarCountAfter` to `coreSidecarResumeCountAfter`; `test-live-soak-prep.mjs` guards live harness proof against `coreSidecars.length || legacySidecarCount`; and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the flush helper against reintroducing `sidecarJournalDelta` into primary progress. Adjacent proof passed for `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs` and `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Active-save sidecar diagnostics fallback removal, July 3: `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` no longer serializes old `runtimeTracking.sidecarJournal` rows into active-save diagnostics segments when CORE sidecar/background projections are absent. `sidecarDiagnosticRows(...)` and `backgroundBatchRows(...)` now fail closed to `[]` unless `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.sidecarDiagnostics` or `backgroundBatches` exist, so stale old sidecar rows cannot inflate diagnostics, materialized-save evidence, or 5000-message save size. - Generic state commit/restore model-call demotion, July 3: `state-delta-gateway.mjs` now uses CORE `modelCallDiagnostics` when carrying model-call telemetry through ordinary state commits and `restoreTrackedCampaignRevision(...)`, falling back to compact old telemetry only when no CORE diagnostics exist. - Model-call pending replay projection dedupe, July 3: `model-call-journal.mjs` now reads CORE `modelCallDiagnostics` when computing the max model-call event sequence and when deduplicating pending fallback telemetry before appending to old `runtimeTracking.modelCallJournal`. This prevents a reload/replay path from duplicating compact model-call rows into the old journal when the same call already exists as a CORE diagnostic projection. - Model-call fallback metadata hardening, July 3: the remaining no-CORE `runtimeTracking.modelCallJournal` fallback is now compact telemetry only. `recordModelCallEvent(...)` no longer persists arbitrary `event.metadata`, so raw prompts, raw responses, provider bodies, request objects, or extension payloads cannot be smuggled into old model-call rows through metadata while CORE diagnostics are still rolling out. - Model-call init/load telemetry compaction, July 3: `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` now sanitizes imported old `runtimeTracking.modelCallJournal` rows through a fixed compact field list instead of cloning raw rows. - Runtime ledger init/load payload compaction, July 3: `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` now compacts imported old `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger` and `responseLedger` rows instead of cloning raw bridge payloads. Existing bridge rows keep ids, status, hashes, timing, projection refs, source-frame refs, host-observation hashes, and other compact structure, but raw `text`, `prompt`, `response`, `metadata`, provider payloads, message arrays, request bodies, transcripts, observed text, selected text, and raw replacement bodies are stripped recursively. `replacementText` is converted to presence/hash/length during init/load. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` imports raw ingress, source-frame, response, host-observation, host-continuation, metadata, and replacement canaries and verifies no raw payload survives while compact hashes remain. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against raw ingress/response cloneback. - Runtime history init/load compaction, July 3: `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` now compacts imported old `runtimeTracking.history` entries instead of cloning raw retained snapshots forward. Entry-level raw prompt/metadata payloads are stripped, and each imported `history[].snapshot` is normalized through `createCampaignStateSnapshot(...)`, so transient `directiveRuntimeEvidence`, `runtimeResume`, nested history, old ingress/response/recovery ledgers, sidecars, model-call telemetry, and pending interactions do not keep multiplying through unrelated state commits. Compact restorable campaign/mission state remains available for the short legacy history window while CORE/v2 checkpoint artifacts own durable replay/rollback. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` imports raw history-entry, nested snapshot, ledger, sidecar, model-call, and pending-interaction canaries and verifies no raw history payload survives while mission state remains restorable. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against raw history cloneback. No live soak lanes were run; routine live validation remains one non-human ST user/lane until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled for certification.
- History-limit and scene-ledger snapshot demotion, July 3: `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` now ignores old imported `runtimeTracking.historyLimit`, uses the active/default retention policy instead, bounds imported old history immediately, and clamps old `historyIndex` after trimming. `runtime-app.updateRuntimeSettings(...)` no longer falls back to old `runtimeTracking.historyLimit` when settings are absent, so stale saves cannot silently preserve 60-entry retained-history behavior after the scale default moved to 8. `createCampaignStateSnapshot(...)` now also strips `lifecycleJournal`, `sceneReconciliation`, and `sceneHandshake` from retained history snapshots, closing the next save-growth path where SRE scene ledger arrays could be duplicated into every legacy turn snapshot. A one-explorer audit identified full scene-ledger ownership migration as the next larger Wave 3 seam; that seam was later closed by moving Scene Reconciliation and Scene Handshake to top-level owner roots and treating nested runtimeTracking copies as empty compatibility shells. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` covers old 60-limit import collapse, explicit 6-limit override, bounded history indexes, and scene/lifecycle raw canaries missing from snapshots. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against reintroducing old history-limit fallback or scene-ledger snapshot retention. Adjacent proof passed for `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs`, `test-campaign-sidecar-scheduler.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, and `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; routine live validation remains one non-human ST user/lane until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled for certification.
- Scene Reconciliation top-level SRE ledger migration, July 3: Scene Reconciliation no longer writes authoritative ledger state to `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation`. `DIRECTIVE_MUTABLE_STATE_DOMAINS` now includes top-level `sceneReconciliation`; after the July 4 owner-root cut, `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` reads only top-level scene owner input and resets nested runtimeTracking scene ledgers to empty compatibility shells. Production runtime/service/UI/settler/proof surfaces read top-level `sceneReconciliation` only. `writeLedger(...)` writes `path: "sceneReconciliation"` and top-level scene-ledger writes do not advance mechanics revisions, avoiding self-staling pending SRE proposals. Director/open-world invalidation helpers also append scene invalidation records to top-level `sceneReconciliation`, not the old runtimeTracking bag. Focused proof in `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs` now asserts top-level ownership while runtimeTracking stays compatibility-only; `test-scene-reconciliation-open-world.mjs` proves pending apply, recalculation preview, accepted/cancelled recalculation, and invalidation flows against the top-level ledger with CORE checkpoint evidence; `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` now proves old tagged runtimeTracking scene ledgers do not promote; and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against reintroducing `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` writes. Adjacent proof passed for `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, and the Ashes campaign projection validator. Remaining migration work is live message-action artifact proof, not production Scene Reconciliation service ownership.
- Scene Reconciliation projection contract cleanup, July 3: bundled campaign projections now expose `sceneReconciliation` as a top-level state domain and no longer package it under `initialState.runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation`. `campaignProjectionStateDomains`, `campaign-state-projection.schema.json`, and `validate-campaign-projection.mjs` now require top-level `sceneReconciliation`; the validator rejects nested packaged scene ledgers so new bundles cannot reintroduce runtimeTracking scene authority. All six bundled campaign projections were mechanically migrated. Focused proof passed for `validate-campaign-projection.mjs` across all bundled projections, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-continuity-projection-foundation.mjs`, syntax checks for validator/skeleton, and a structural scan that verified every bundled projection includes top-level `sceneReconciliation` and no nested runtimeTracking copy. Remaining Scene Reconciliation work is live message-action artifact proof, not packaged projection ownership.

- Response-dispatcher diagnostic-only mirror removal, July 3: CORE response/continuity writer failures still append compact CORE diagnostics and duplicate dispatch still detects those diagnostics without reposting/releasing, but `response-dispatcher.mjs` no longer mints or updates old `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` rows from diagnostic-only evidence when no real CORE response projection exists. `findExisting(...)` can synthesize an ephemeral duplicate entry from CORE `sidecarDiagnostics`, while `recordDirectiveResponseCompatibility(...)` and `updateDirectiveResponseCompatibility(...)` leave old responseLedger state unchanged for `coreDiagnostic`-only paths. - State-delta quarantine escape removal, July 3: `recordTurnIngress(...)`, `updateTurnIngress(...)`, `recordDirectiveResponse(...)`, and `updateDirectiveResponse(...)` no longer allow missing-CORE old-ledger writes through `missingCoreWriteMode: "quarantine"`. Missing CORE projection evidence now always raises `DIRECTIVE_CORE_PROJECTION_REQUIRED_FOR_OLD_LEDGER_WRITE`; tests that need legacy rows must import them as existing runtime state or provide CORE transaction/projection evidence. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` verifies new missing-CORE ingress writes and imported silent ingress/response updates fail even when old quarantine mode is requested, while timing/replacement-text fixtures still pass with CORE evidence and raw replacement text remains hash-only. `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against reintroducing the quarantine escape hatch. - Old-ledger explicit-authority bypass closure, July 3: state-delta ingress/response writers no longer treat a caller-supplied `authority: "compatibilityProjection"` or other non-`compatibilityProjectionUnavailable` authority string as sufficient evidence for mutating old `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger` / `responseLedger` mirrors. `assertMissingCoreWriteAllowed(...)` now requires actual CORE projection evidence, compatibility mirror evidence, CORE transaction/recovery/release/completion evidence, or diagnostics-backed projection data before old mirror mutation; forged authority strings with `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"` still fail with `DIRECTIVE_CORE_PROJECTION_REQUIRED_FOR_OLD_LEDGER_WRITE`. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` covers forged ingress and response rows, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards against restoring the explicit-authority return bypass. - Model-call old-ledger fallback removal, July 3: `createRuntimeModelCallJournal(...)` no longer appends pending model calls into `runtimeTracking.modelCallJournal` when a CORE diagnostic target is unavailable. Runtime model-call events now either become CORE diagnostics or advance only the compact `runtimeResume.modelCallEventSequence` cursor; direct `recordModelCallEvent(...)` old-ledger writes reject with `DIRECTIVE_CORE_PROJECTION_REQUIRED_FOR_OLD_LEDGER_WRITE` unless a test/import path explicitly opts into legacy telemetry. Review-only model calls still return compact non-durable generation proof for live/model-review artifacts, but durable deltas and proof-facing counts are CORE-only. Directive Assist and Command Bearing live-fixture helpers now preserve cursor evidence without old journal growth, and Command Bearing readied/cancelled view refreshes sync current-chat scope before rendering. - Pending-interaction authority gate, July 3: `recordPendingInteraction(...)` and `resolvePendingInteraction(...)` now require explicit owner evidence before mutating old `runtimeTracking.pendingInteractions`. Valid owners are CORE pending-interaction projection, terminal decision projection, REPAIR pending-interaction projection, or explicitly marked legacy telemetry for import/test-only paths; missing authority raises `DIRECTIVE_PENDING_INTERACTION_AUTHORITY_REQUIRED`. Chat-turn pause creation now derives a compact `directive.corePendingInteractionProjectionRef.v1` from the active ingress CORE transaction, terminal end-condition detection records a compact `directive.terminalPendingInteractionProjectionRef.v1`, and terminal test fixtures use terminal projection evidence instead of silent old rows. Untagged pending-interaction rows can no longer supply CORE target ids for terminal checkpoint control batches, even if they are present in an unnormalized runtime object. Decision rows preserve owner metadata across checkpoint posting, replay, Push On, Keep Ending, and Save Branch updates, and branch rows record terminal projection evidence instead of being silent old-ledger writes. This does not yet globally reject imported/no-owner `endConditionLedger` rows, but it removes the production service's authority-free terminal decision/branch writes and gives the next demotion slice a clean owner field to enforce. Active terminal decision ids survive only when the matching tagged pending decision survives. `applyPushOnContinuationFrame(...)` now tags continuation-frame status rows with compact `directive.terminalEndConditionLedgerProjectionRef.v1` owner evidence, and hand-built terminal test fixtures now use terminal projection rows rather than silent old ledgers.
