The physical compatibility mirror still lives under `runtimeTracking.endConditionLedger` for now, but production reads/writes require compact `directive.terminalEndConditionLedgerProjectionRef.v1` owner evidence. Focused proof passed for syntax checks on touched runtime/campaign modules plus `test-campaign-end-condition-service.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-terminal-outcome.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Terminal read/restore consumer closeout, July 3: REPAIR rollback restore and Mission UI terminal controls now also use `terminalDecisionLedgerView(...)` rather than raw `runtimeTracking.endConditionLedger`. Untagged imported/no-owner terminal detections, decisions, branch records, and continuation frames therefore cannot survive rollback restore, populate terminal action buttons, or inflate saved-branch counts. Focused proof passed for syntax on `repair-command-boundary.mjs` and `mission-panel.js`, `test-end-condition-ui-contracts.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; adjacent proof passed for `test-campaign-end-condition-service.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-terminal-outcome.mjs`, and `test-message-recovery.mjs`. No live ST lane was run because this is deterministic terminal projection behavior.
- Open-world nested Scene Reconciliation fallback removal, July 3: open-world initialization, coordinator snapshots, and reaction-engine event invalidation no longer promote old nested `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` rows into top-level `sceneReconciliation` SRE authority. Top-level Scene Reconciliation remains the only open-world SRE ledger source; old nested rows are ignored rather than migrated by director/reaction paths. Focused proof in `test-open-world-director-coordinator-contracts.mjs` covers nested-only rows not migrating or counting, top-level pending rows still counting, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards director/reaction source against reintroducing the fallback. Syntax checks passed for `director-coordinator.mjs` and `reaction-engine.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- CORE-authoritative history snapshot ref cut, July 3: `commitTrackedCampaignState(...)` no longer stores full `runtimeTracking.history[*].snapshot` bodies when current runtime evidence declares `runtimeAuthority: "coreStoreV2"`. CORE-authoritative commits now store a compact `directive.coreRuntimeHistorySnapshotRef.v1` with revision/turn/outcome ids and fail closed through `restoreTrackedCampaignRevision(...)` until a CORE checkpoint restore path is selected. Legacy/no-CORE tracked commits still keep compact old snapshots for transitional restore coverage. This directly reduces save-growth on v2-authoritative runtime paths and prevents old snapshot bodies from remaining hidden restore authority after CORE owns the runtime. Focused proof in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` covers CORE-authoritative history refs, old restore fail-closed behavior, and hot-overlay commit preservation without snapshot authority; `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards the commit helper shape. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Terminal checkpoint lastCommittedTurn fallback removal, July 3: terminal end-condition detection and terminal replay checkpoint selection no longer use `runtimeTracking.lastCommittedTurn.coreCheckpointRef` as replay authority. The only accepted terminal replay checkpoint source is a compact CORE checkpoint ref attached to the relevant `turnLedger.entries[]` row, which the turn-commit coordinator already annotates after CORE mechanics commit. `lastCommittedTurn` may still mirror turn status for UI/runtime progress, but it cannot authorize terminal replay if the turn ledger lacks CORE checkpoint evidence. Focused proof in `test-end-condition-evaluator.mjs` and `test-campaign-end-condition-service.mjs` covers stale `lastCommittedTurn` checkpoint refs being ignored; `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` guards both terminal source files. `test-turn-commit-coordinator-core-mechanics.mjs` now seeds its base ingress as an explicit CORE ingress projection rather than a silent old row, keeping the adjacent CORE checkpoint annotation gate aligned with old-ledger demotion. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Latest-pair SRE owner fallback closure, July 3: `source-settlement-latest-pair.mjs` now calls the strict `runLatestPairSourceSettlement(...)` entrypoint instead of the legacy `runSceneHandshakeSettlement(...)` entrypoint. The strict entrypoint still uses the shared Scene Handshake snapshot/ledger machinery, and the later owner-root cut moved physical rows to top-level `sceneHandshake`; it disables legacy `sceneHandshakeSettler` fallback when the latest-pair provider is unavailable, so missing owner/provider state returns a compact `repairRequired` source-settlement decision instead of silently invoking the old role. This preserved the existing Scene Handshake behavior while tightening SRE ownership of the production latest-pair path. Focused proof passed for `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs` with a no-provider strict latest-pair regression, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` production routing, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and syntax checks for `scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, `source-settlement-latest-pair.mjs`, and the Scene Handshake test. No live soak lanes were run; routine live proof remains one non-human `directive-soak-*` user until broad certification is re-enabled.
- Runtime boundary/time metadata authority demotion, July 3: `runtimeTracking.lastWorldBoundary` and `runtimeTracking.timeNormalization` now require compact owner projection evidence on import/load/commit normalization. Director coordinator writes `lastWorldBoundary` with `openWorldBoundaryProjection`, `projectionSource: "directorCoordinator"`, and `directive.openWorldBoundaryProjectionRef.v1`; campaign time normalization writes `timeNormalizationProjection`, `projectionSource: "campaignTimeState"`, and `directive.timeNormalizationProjectionRef.v1`. `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` drops unowned imported boundary/time metadata, and open-world reducer validation rejects unowned `runtimeTracking.lastWorldBoundary` / `runtimeTracking.timeNormalization` operations so replay bundles cannot revive raw legacy metadata. Focused proof passed for `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-open-world-director-coordinator-contracts.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, and syntax checks for touched runtime/director/time modules. One read-only explorer ranked the next larger implementation slice as terminal decision ledger ownership: `runtimeTracking.endConditionLedger` rows are tagged/demoted, but terminal detection/decision/branch/continuation records still need a CORE/REPAIR-owned projection accessor before that mirror is compatibility-only.
- Lifecycle-journal authority gate, July 3: `recordLifecycleEvent(...)` now requires runtime or REPAIR owner evidence before appending old `runtimeTracking.lifecycleJournal` rows. Missing evidence raises `DIRECTIVE_LIFECYCLE_AUTHORITY_REQUIRED`; valid rows store compact `directive.lifecycleCompatibilityMirror.v1` metadata plus the caller's projection evidence. Runtime-app campaign difficulty changes and chat rebinding now pass compact `directive.runtimeLifecycleProjectionRef.v1` refs, while generic state revision restore is tagged as a REPAIR lifecycle projection. Focused proof passed in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-campaign-difficulty-runtime.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Lifecycle-journal demotion, July 3: `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` now drops untagged old `runtimeTracking.lifecycleJournal` rows and explicit `legacyLifecycleTelemetry` rows instead of letting imported lifecycle telemetry survive as runtime authority. Only rows with `runtimeLifecycleProjection` or `repairLifecycleProjection` plus compact `directive.lifecycleCompatibilityMirror.v1` owner evidence survive initialization/load/commit normalization. Focused proof passed in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs` and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; adjacent chat-native proof still passed after the demotion. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Scene ledger authority tags, July 3: before the top-level SRE cutover, service-created Scene Reconciliation ledger snapshots were tagged with compact `directive.sceneReconciliationLedgerProjectionRef.v1` SRE owner evidence at the ledger root, and early Scene Handshake settlement records all carried compact `directive.sceneHandshakeLedgerProjectionRef.v1` row evidence through `sceneHandshakeLedgerRecord(...)`. This authority-tag slice was superseded by the later top-level owner and latest-pair authority split: Scene Reconciliation proof/runtime surfaces must not certify nested runtimeTracking SRE rows as live state, and legacy `sceneHandshakeSettler` rows now carry `legacySceneHandshakeSettlerTelemetry` instead of SRE owner projection evidence. Focused proof passed in `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs` and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; syntax checks passed for both touched runtime modules. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Scene ledger demotion, July 3: `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` first dropped untagged old `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` roots and untagged `runtimeTracking.sceneHandshake` rows/lastResult instead of letting imported scene-review status become runtime authority. At that point tagged Scene Reconciliation and Scene Handshake rows could survive only with compact SRE projection evidence; the later July 4 owner-root cut superseded that bridge, so nested tagged rows no longer promote and live scene state must arrive through top-level `sceneReconciliation` or `sceneHandshake`. `normalizeSceneReconciliationState(...)` now preserves root owner metadata across service reads/writes, and the changed-range completion path merges previously interrupted SRE runs back into the final ledger after safe state applies so interruption audit rows are not lost during reconciliation. Focused proof passed in `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; adjacent proof passed in `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`; syntax checks passed for `state-delta-gateway.mjs` and `scene-reconciliation.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run; current live policy remains one non-human ST user/lane for targeted validation until broad soak is explicitly re-enabled.
- Correct-as-Swipe response backfill removal, July 3: a one-agent production old-ledger audit found no remaining `recordRecoveryEvent` production hits and identified `runtime-app.mjs` Correct-as-Swipe candidate/lifecycle response backfill as the smallest remaining visible response-ledger debt. `proposeCorrectAsSwipeCandidate(...)` and `settleCorrectAsSwipeCase(...)` now fail closed with `DIRECTIVE_CORRECT_AS_SWIPE_RESPONSE_PROJECTION_REQUIRED` unless a CORE/tagged response projection row already exists, instead of minting an old `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` row from app payload data. Focused proof passed for `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-correct-as-swipe-workflow.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- CORE bounded mechanics guard, July 3: `core-store-v2.commitMechanics(...)` now rejects unbounded mechanics operation payloads before mechanics revision, event append, turn append, checkpoint write, or manifest write. Mechanics operations may carry bounded refs/hashes such as domain, op, source hash, changed roots, operation count, and value hash; they may not carry raw text, prompt/provider/vector/secret fields, full `campaignState`, `rootsSet`, snapshot payloads, generic payloads, or literal `value` bodies. This narrows the remaining Stage 11 mechanics gap: CORE still receives the compatibility-derived mechanics bundle from `turn-commit-coordinator`, but the durable CORE event/turn path can no longer silently compact an unbounded operation payload into authority. Focused proof passed: `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-turn-commit-coordinator-core-mechanics.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.

- Response lifecycle projection evidence guard, July 3: Outcome Integrity and Correct-as-Swipe runtime lifecycle patches can no longer mint CORE-looking `runtimeTracking.responseLedger` compatibility rows unless the response carries real transaction evidence from `coreTransactionId`, `transactionId`, an existing CORE projection, compatibility mirror, recovery, release, or completion ref. `responseCompatibilityProjectionPatch(...)` now fails closed with `DIRECTIVE_CORE_RESPONSE_PROJECTION_TRANSACTION_REQUIRED` before old response-ledger mutation when the lifecycle patch would otherwise write `projectionSource: "coreStoreV2"` with a null transaction id. This removes a remaining Wave 1/Wave 2 split-brain path where response lifecycle metadata could look CORE-owned while lacking CORE transaction authority. Focused proof passed: `test-correct-as-swipe-workflow.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.

- Model-call CORE diagnostics primary cut, July 3: `createRuntimeModelCallJournal(...)` now classifies model calls with a resolved CORE diagnostic transaction as CORE-diagnostic-primary. Those calls enqueue bounded `modelCall` diagnostics into CORE, advance only the compact `runtimeResume.modelCallEventSequence` cursor in runtime state, and skip appending matching rows to old `runtimeTracking.modelCallJournal`. Calls without a CORE transaction still use the compact legacy journal fallback, so startup/background model calls that cannot yet attach to a turn remain diagnosable without inventing CORE authority. `chatNativeViewForState(...)` now exposes the compact model-call cursor separately from old journal count. Focused proof passed: `test-runtime-model-call-journal.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- Production LENS external-context observer cut, July 3: `runtime-app.mjs` no longer gives LENS a null external prompt-environment observer. Prompt sync and activation now read compact metadata from the public prompt adapter `inspect({ includeText: false })`, pass the adapter-style bundle into `lens.flush(...)`, and keep the existing ref fallback only when inspection is unavailable. Fresh prompt-adapter refs win over stale source-frame refs for LENS cache identity, while adapter target summaries carry current ST Lorebooks/World Info, Memory Books, Summaryception, VectFox, and unknown external-context evidence into budget traces. `lens-prompt-scheduler.mjs` now preserves adapter-provided `externalPromptEnvironmentRef`, `knownExternalPromptKeys`, and `externalPromptEnvironmentTargets` instead of normalizing target summaries away, without raw prompt/vector/provider content and without calling private extension internals. Focused proof added an adapter-style inspection flush in `test-prompt-dirty-domains.mjs`, static owner guards in `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and chat-native runtime proof in `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs` that a stale frame ref cannot override a fresh prompt-adapter ref. No live soak lanes were run.
- External-context latency attribution cut, July 3: the SillyTavern external-context observer now carries compact `timingDiagnostics` for ST Lorebooks/World Info, Memory Books, and Summaryception in addition to the existing VectFox backend timing diagnostics. The normalized environment, diagnostic evidence hashes, target summaries, LENS cache inputs, and live summary artifacts can now attribute bounded prompt-composition, scan, retrieval, summary, and interceptor timing without storing raw timing payloads, raw prompts, generated memories, summaries, vector hits, embeddings, or secrets. Focused proof in `test-sillytavern-external-context-observer.mjs` covers per-target timing diagnostics and redaction canaries; adjacent proof passed for `test-lens-prompt-budget-lane-contracts.mjs`, `test-prompt-dirty-domains.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-external-context-readiness-preflight.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live soak lanes were run.
- External-context timing summary gate, July 3: `external-context-summary.json` now aggregates `timingCoverage` from target summaries, writes the compact timing coverage into the artifact and live-log artifact record, and the Continuity Matrix lane completeness check fails if the summary omits the timing coverage field or has no timed target evidence. The gate does not require all four targets to have measured latency yet because disabled/unavailable tools may not expose timing, but it makes total absence of latency attribution non-certifying and reports `targetsMissingTiming` for final proof review. Focused proof passed in `test-external-context-summary-artifact.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, and `test-continuity-matrix-five-user-soak-coordinator.mjs`; adjacent readiness and observer proof still passed. No live soak lanes were run.
- Mission stateDelta CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: `turn-commit-coordinator` now promotes non-empty `turnPacket.stateDelta.mission` into a compact `stateDeltaCommitted` CORE mechanics operation with source kind, outcome id, source hash, and committed root hash, then excludes `mission` from broad `domainCommitted` fallback for that turn. This was the first explicit domain promotion; later bullets record the additional audited domain and packet promotions. Focused proof in `test-turn-commit-coordinator-core-mechanics.mjs` verifies mission no longer uses broad domain source; adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- CommandCulture stateDelta CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: the same explicit CORE mechanics source path now includes `turnPacket.stateDelta.commandCulture` when it is non-empty and actually changes the committed root. This is a bounded hash/ref promotion only; CORE does not store tendency text in the mechanics event. Focused proof in `test-turn-commit-coordinator-core-mechanics.mjs` verifies commandCulture uses `stateDeltaCommitted` and no longer uses broad `domainCommitted`; adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- PressureLedger stateDelta CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: `turnPacket.stateDelta.pressureLedger` now joins the explicit CORE mechanics source allowlist. This is safe as a hash-only source promotion because `applyPressureLedgerDelta(...)` maps to the `pressureLedger` root, while the CORE mechanics operation stores source hash and committed root hash only. Focused proof adds a raw pressure-text canary to the delta and committed state, verifies `pressureLedger` uses `stateDeltaCommitted` instead of broad `domainCommitted`, and verifies the raw pressure text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- CommandBearing stateDelta CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: `turnPacket.stateDelta.commandBearing` now joins the explicit CORE mechanics source allowlist for direct turn mechanics changes. The audit confirmed `applyCommandBearingDelta(...)` maps this turn delta to the `commandBearing` root; sidecar Command Bearing evidence/review settlements remain their separate FORGE/CORE paths. The CORE mechanics operation stores only stateDelta source hash and committed root hash, not award/evidence/review text. Focused proof adds a raw Command Bearing text canary to the delta and committed state, verifies `commandBearing` uses `stateDeltaCommitted` instead of broad `domainCommitted`, and verifies the raw Command Bearing text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- CommandCompetence competencePacket CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: direct turn Command Competence changes now use an explicit `competencePacketCommitted` CORE mechanics operation sourced from `turnPacket.competencePacket`, with source kind, outcome id, packet hash, and committed root hash. The audit confirmed `applyCommandCompetenceRecords(...)` appends direct turn competence ledgers from the packet into the `commandCompetence` root; unrelated utility/state-commit paths remain outside this source promotion. Focused proof adds a raw competence routine-action canary to the packet and committed state, verifies `commandCompetence` no longer uses broad `domainCommitted` for this path, and verifies the raw competence text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- CommandLog and TurnLedger packet CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: direct command-log and turn-ledger appends now use explicit packet-sourced CORE mechanics operations instead of broad root fallback. `commandLogPacketCommitted` is sourced from `turnPacket.commandLogPacket`; `turnLedgerEntryCommitted` is sourced from the turn packet fields that `appendLedgerEntry(...)` commits. Both operations store only source hashes and committed root hashes, not summary inputs, visible consequences, competence packet prose, stateDelta prose, or narration text. Focused proof adds raw command-log and turn-ledger packet canaries, verifies `commandLog` and `turnLedger` no longer use broad `domainCommitted`, and verifies the raw packet text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. A one-agent audit identified the next safe candidates as custom `worldState` sub-ops for `stateDelta.actors`/`stateDelta.fronts`, then `stateDelta.clocks`; later bullets record those cuts plus terminal-state and relationship source separation. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- WorldState subdelta CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: the audited `stateDelta.actors`, `stateDelta.fronts`, and `stateDelta.clocks` paths now emit custom hash-only worldState source operations: `actorPosturesCommitted`, `frontRecordsCommitted`, and `clockDeltasCommitted`. These are not generic root promotions; they point to the exact packet subdelta path, carry source kind/outcome/source hash/operation count/changed root/value hash, and can coexist with `openWorld` reducer evidence when the same turn changes `worldState`. Focused proof adds raw actor posture, front record, and clock reason canaries, verifies all three worldState subdelta operations are present, verifies broad `worldState domainCommitted` stays absent when reducer/subdelta sources explain the root, and verifies raw worldState subdelta text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- TerminalState CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: the cross-root `stateDelta.terminalState` path now emits one bounded source operation per touched root: `shipTerminalStateCommitted`, `playerTerminalStateCommitted`, and `flagsTerminalStateCommitted`. This deliberately does not collapse terminal state into a single generic root commit: each operation names the exact subpath (`shipPatch`, `playerPatch`, or `flagsSet`), stores source kind/outcome/source hash/operation count/value hash only, and replaces broad `ship domainCommitted` while adding explicit `player` and `flags` source evidence that the old MECHANICS_DOMAINS fallback did not cover. Focused proof adds raw ship/player/flag terminal canaries, verifies all three terminal operations are present, verifies broad ship fallback is gone, and verifies raw terminal-state text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- Relationships CORE mechanics source cut, July 3: the last obvious focused-fixture fallback now splits `relationships` into two bounded source operations instead of a broad `domainCommitted` root diff. `relationshipStateDeltaCommitted` records the direct `stateDelta.relationships` source hash for descriptive/perception ledger changes; `relationshipMemoryDerivedCommitted` records the derived `applyRelationshipMemoryFromTurn(...)` source hash, scoped crew count, and committed memory-ledger hash. This preserves the distinction that originally made relationships unsafe to promote: packet changes and hidden derived memory are separate sources, both hash-only. Focused proof adds raw relationship and relationship-memory canaries, verifies both operations are present, verifies broad `relationships domainCommitted` is gone, and verifies raw relationship text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- Compatibility mechanics fallback demotion, July 3: residual broad `domainCommitted` operations are no longer silent root-diff authority. They now carry `sourceKind: directive.compatibilityMechanicsDomainFallback.v1`, source outcome id, source hash derived from domain + before/after hashes, and committed root hash. The fully sourced turn fixture now asserts that no `domainCommitted` operations are needed after explicit mission, command culture, pressure, Command Bearing, Command Competence, command log, turn ledger, worldState subdelta, terminal-state, relationship, and open-world reducer sources explain the changed roots. A separate fallback fixture changes only `values`, proves the remaining fallback stays hash-only and compatibility-labeled, and proves raw fallback text does not enter the CORE mechanics bundle. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- Durable CORE mechanics source-ref compaction, July 3: CORE Store now retains bounded `sourceTurnId` on compact mechanics operations, so source refs emitted by the turn coordinator survive durable event/turn compaction instead of existing only in the pre-store bundle. `test-core-store-v2.mjs` now proves a reducer/source operation persists `sourceKind`, `sourceHash`, `sourceOutcomeId`, `sourceTurnId`, operation count, changed roots, and value hash, and that a compatibility fallback operation persists `sourceKind: directive.compatibilityMechanicsDomainFallback.v1`, domain/op, source hash, outcome id, and value hash. The same proof keeps raw mechanics/checkpoint text out of event and turn projections. Adjacent proof passed for `test-turn-commit-coordinator-core-mechanics.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`.
- Open-world old-ledger stale mutation removal, July 3: `invalidateOpenWorldCausalityForReconciliation(...)` no longer marks old `runtimeTracking.responseLedger`, `runtimeTracking.sidecarJournal`, or `runtimeTracking.modelCallJournal` rows stale in place. Source invalidation evidence now stays in event/thread/quest ledgers and top-level Scene Reconciliation invalidations, preventing old runtime journals from regaining durable authority while SRE/REPAIR owns source mutation review. Focused proof passed for `test-open-world-director-coordinator-contracts.mjs`, `test-open-world-event-reducers.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; syntax proof passed for `src/directors/director-coordinator.mjs`; `git diff --check` passed with CRLF warnings only. No live ST lane was run because this slice is deterministic; if later live proof is needed, the active budget is one explicit non-human `directive-soak-*` user.
- Scene Reconciliation live fallback removal, July 3: production SRE/UI/settler surfaces no longer let nested `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` drive live behavior. `sceneReconciliationState(...)`, `runtime-app` chatNative view, Mission panel recovery review, and Scene Handshake safety snapshots now read top-level `sceneReconciliation` only. The later owner-root cut removed the remaining state-delta bridge promotion for tagged nested SRE ledgers, so raw or tagged nested runtime ledgers cannot directly create pending review cards, settler safety counts, service state, or top-level owner rows. Focused proof passed for `test-scene-reconciliation.mjs`, `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, `test-end-condition-ui-contracts.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and adjacent `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`; syntax proof passed for `scene-reconciliation.mjs`, `runtime-app.mjs`, `scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, and `mission-panel.js`. `git diff --check` passed with CRLF warnings only, and hot-file grep found no remaining `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` fallback in the touched production surfaces. No live ST lane was run because this slice is deterministic; if a later live check is needed, use one explicit non-human `directive-soak-*` user.
- Scene Reconciliation live-proof fallback removal, July 3: Wave 4 proof tooling now matches the production SRE ownership cut. `sillytavern-live-harness.mjs`, `run-sillytavern-message-action-live.mjs`, `run-sillytavern-message-edit-live.mjs`, and `run-sillytavern-message-delete-live.mjs` report Scene Reconciliation evidence from top-level `campaignState.sceneReconciliation` only, so standalone message-action and mutation child artifacts cannot pass or display stale nested `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` as live SRE state. `test-live-soak-prep.mjs` now guards the live harness, message-action runner, and stale plan phrases; `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs` guards edit/delete child reporters. Focused proof passed: syntax checks for all three live runners plus `sillytavern-live-harness.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; targeted grep found no proof-runner `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` fallback in the main live harness/message-action/edit/delete files. No live ST lane was run because this is proof-source alignment; use one explicit non-human `directive-soak-*` user for the next targeted live rehearsal.
- Live proof-source fallback removal, July 3: smoke and child proof tooling no longer lets raw `runtimeTracking` satisfy live certification paths. `smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs` now reads ingress/response/recovery through authoritative CORE runtime ledger projections, uses `terminalDecisionLedgerView(...)` for terminal summaries, keeps raw sidecar rows only as `legacySidecarCount`, and requires CORE ingress proof for any sent round. Runtime send progress now reports `core-runtime-ledger*` sources instead of `runtime-tracking*`; visible/direct-observe evidence remains separate. `sillytavern-live-harness.mjs` and `run-sillytavern-message-action-live.mjs` now expose generic `recoveryCount`/`recentRecoveryJournal` from CORE projection evidence while old rows stay legacy-named. The live run exposed a production view gap: CORE events were written, but `getCurrentView(...)` did not attach fresh CORE read projections soon enough for proof tooling. `runtime-app.mjs` now refreshes active and current-chat view state through `stateWithCoreProjectionFreshnessEvidence(...)` before returning the runtime view, keeping smoke proof CORE-owned without disk hacks or old-ledger fallback. Focused proof passed for `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-message-mutation-actuation-live-runner.mjs`, syntax checks for touched live scripts and `runtime-app.mjs`, and a one-user Playwright live run after syncing only `directive-soak-b`: `artifacts/live-smoke/proof-source-single-user-generation-final-2026-07-03/report.json`. That run created fresh Ashes save `save-1783127034586-3-2ab0bb12`, sent one player message, and passed CORE generation timing (`coreStoreTurnTiming`/`coreProjection`), CORE ingress (`coreStoreIngressLedger`), and CORE host-native completion (`coreStoreResponseLedger`/`coreProjection`) with no `default-user` and no five-lane soak.
- CORE response edit/source recovery demotion, July 3: chat-native sidecar flush now exposes old sidecar journal counters only as `legacySidecarJournal*` telemetry while primary deltas come from CORE resume/diagnostic counts. Outcome Integrity accepted prose edits no longer depend on old response-ledger rows after CORE-only projection refresh: the accepted edit path writes compact `outcomeIntegrity` evidence through `repairVisibleResponseRef(...)`, CORE response projections carry selected revision/hash evidence without raw replacement text, and runtime tests assert the CORE projection shape instead of `runtimeTracking.responseLedger`. Chat-turn duplicate detection now uses async CORE-first ingress ledger reads so reobserved player messages dedupe after old ingress rows disappear. REPAIR source-mutation transaction lookup now accepts CORE projection `transactionId` aliases as well as old `coreTransactionId`, allowing post-reload source edits to record CORE recovery projections without old recovery-journal writes. Focused proof passed for `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-repair-runtime.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. One-user Playwright proof passed after syncing only `directive-soak-b`: `artifacts/live-smoke/core-response-edit-demotion-hostcontinue-single-user-2026-07-03/report.json`. That run created fresh Ashes campaign `campaign-1783130867939-2-dbb43c01`, save `save-1783130867939-3-fd70f63c`, chat `Directive - Ashes of Peace (88) - 2026-07-03@20h07m48s642ms`, sent one player message, and passed CORE generation timing (`coreStoreTurnTiming`/`coreProjection`) plus CORE host-native completion (`coreStoreResponseLedger`/`coreProjection`) with `directive-soak-b`, no `default-user`, and no five-lane soak.
- Witness-scope LENS evidence cut, July 3: continuity fact budget refs now carry compact `knowledgeScope` evidence (`knownBy`, `witnessedBy`, `subjectIds`, `disclosureState`, and `disclosureSourceFrameId`) so active-cast/protected-continuity LENS traces can prove why a scoped fact was prompt-eligible without preserving raw fact text. `lens-prompt-scheduler.mjs` and `lens-prompt-budget-trace.mjs` preserve only that compact scope digest in included/omitted/over-budget refs, and raw witness/fact text remains stripped. Focused proof passed for `test-continuity-projection-foundation.mjs` and `test-lens-prompt-budget-lane-contracts.mjs`; adjacent proof passed for `test-player-safe-prompt-context.mjs`, `test-continuity-director-packets.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`. No live ST lane was run for this deterministic prompt-budget evidence slice; live certification still needs host artifact proof after the remaining Wave 1/2 blockers clear.

- Runtime persistence pending-merge hardening, July 4: queued runtime persistence no longer replaces the single pending request with the latest snapshot alone. `runtime-app.mjs` now merges same save/chat/campaign pending requests by preserving fresher CORE turn/ingress/response/recovery projections, newer prompt-context revisions, runtime resume sidecar counters, command log growth, pending interaction projections, terminal ledger projections, and max runtime/mechanics revisions before the pending write drains. This keeps coalescing behavior without letting a later stale background prompt/sidecar save drop an earlier queued committed-turn CORE projection, which directly supports the no-hot-full-save and CORE authority goals. Focused RED/GREEN proof added to `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`; `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `node --check src/runtime/runtime-app.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed. Served `directive-soak-b` `runtime-app.mjs` was hash-synced, Playwright readiness passed, and one-user `directive-soak-b` Playwright smoke passed with persisted report `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-runtime-persist-merge/report.json` (`ok: true`, live generation enabled, chat campaign ran, stopped on clarification; generation timing/host completion proof remained warning because no committed host-native turn occurred).

- SRE preflight diagnostic defer hardening, July 4: `source-settlement-service.mjs` now treats preflight CORE diagnostic append as diagnostic-only even when the append rejects immediately, not only when it times out. `appendPreflightDiagnostic(...)` returns compact queued evidence for timeout or write failure, so source preflight remains fast/fail-closed on source integrity rather than failing because a CORE diagnostic side write was slow or unavailable; full evaluate/apply diagnostic writes remain unchanged. Focused RED/GREEN proof in `test-source-reconciliation-engine-synthetic.mjs` covers immediate rejection, >500ms defer timing, and late settlement. `node --check src/runtime/source-settlement-service.mjs`, `test-source-reconciliation-engine-synthetic.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed. Served `directive-soak-b` `source-settlement-service.mjs` was hash-synced, and one-user `directive-soak-b` Playwright smoke passed with persisted report `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-sre-preflight-defer/report.json` (`ok: true`, live generation enabled, chat campaign ran, stopped on clarification; generation timing/host completion proof remained warning because no committed host-native turn occurred).

- Strict live-proof warning rejection, July 4: diagnostic one-user smoke may still finish with `qualityStatus: "warning"` for non-certification runs, but strict/certification smoke now rejects warning-only persisted generation timing or host-native completion proof instead of letting release proof pass on warnings. `smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs` now uses `liveSmokeStrictProofFailures(...)` to require raw persisted CORE generation timing and host-native completion proof statuses to be `pass` when `DIRECTIVE_SILLYTAVERN_STRICT=1`; failure details retain compact proof counts/sources for triage. Focused RED/GREEN proof in `test-live-soak-prep.mjs` covers diagnostic warning allowance and strict warning rejection for both proof lanes, with source-contract coverage that the smoke runner imports and calls the policy. `node --check tools/scripts/smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs`, `node --check tools/scripts/lib/live-smoke-proof-policy.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-smoke-sillytavern-core-proof-targeting.mjs`, `test-generation-timing-proof-policy.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed. One-user `directive-soak-b` non-strict Playwright smoke first exposed provider/host-generation latency at the 180s timeout (`sillytavern-generation-busy`), then passed with a 300s chat timeout at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-strict-proof-policy-rerun/report.json` (`ok: true`, live generation enabled, chat campaign ran, stopped on clarification). This slice deliberately did not run strict live smoke because the current one-user script has no committed host-native turn and would now fail by design until a certification script exercises passable proof.

- Default live-smoke script proof closure, July 4: the built-in one-user smoke chat script no longer starts with the ambiguous `change course and pursue the freighter` order that caused the model to request clarification instead of producing a committed host-native turn. Its default messages now use bounded Ashes command shapes with explicit `target` and `Method` clauses, and the first default turn requires host-native completion proof. This keeps routine one-user smoke aligned with the strict proof policy: if the host cannot produce persisted CORE generation-start and host-native completion evidence for the bounded command, strict mode should fail for a real proof reason rather than because the default fixture asked an underspecified question. Focused RED/GREEN proof in `test-live-soak-prep.mjs` now requires the default source to contain the passive-intercept target/method fixture, requires a host-native completion proof marker, and rejects the old ambiguous pursuit wording. `node tools/scripts/test-live-soak-prep.mjs` and `node --check tools/scripts/smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs` passed; broader deterministic and one-user live proof still need to run for this slice.

- Runtime diagnostics segment churn closure, July 4: the first one-user smoke with the proof-friendly default message proved the prompt fixture fix worked, because SillyTavern produced a normal host-native assistant reply instead of a clarification. It also exposed the save-bloat mechanism behind the earlier "tons of saving" suspicion: after the reply, the smoke runner timed out while the live log showed hundreds of `/api/files/upload` requests, including repeated ~350 KB uploads and dozens of unique `diagnostics-0000-*.v2.json` files. The repeated files contained identical `runtime-persistence-summary` diagnostics; only timestamp/versioned segment ids changed. `transaction-store-v2.mjs` now reuses an existing segment ref when the segment kind, schema, campaign/save ids, entry count, and entry payloads are unchanged, so timestamp-only runtime persistence no longer publishes another diagnostics segment file. Focused RED/GREEN proof in `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs` covers the versioned-tail case: persist diagnostic A, publish changed diagnostic B as a versioned ref, then persist B again and require the same ref with no diagnostics write. `node tools/scripts/test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `node tools/scripts/test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, and `node --check src/storage/transaction-store-v2.mjs` passed. The failed live artifact remains useful evidence at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-default-smoke-script-proof/`: no final `report.json`, transcript reached 3 messages, live log ran from `2026-07-04T07:03:14Z` to `2026-07-04T07:10:38Z`, and persisted files under `directive-soak-b` showed repeated unique runtime-summary diagnostics until the hung smoke process was stopped.

- Host-native completion proof blocker, July 4: after syncing the `transaction-store-v2.mjs` fix into the live `directive-soak-b` served extension copy (`8F5484D04C0249BA3BA5DA4D4D868F5140E396EC648E650EAC860677DA3E3967`) and rerunning one-user Playwright smoke at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-default-smoke-script-proof-rerun/`, the smoke no longer hung and wrote a final failing report. The transcript again reached a real host-native assistant reply for `default-1`, so the default fixture is valid. The failure is now the intended strict proof gap: `Required host-native completion proof was not recorded`, `proofWaitAttempts: 116`, `proofWaitMs: 361642`, `targetOutcomeStatus: "pending"`, `candidateResponseCount: 0`, and `unavailableReason: "no-hostContinue-completion-candidates"`. Persisted file inspection showed diagnostics sidecar count down to 13 total files for the save, with no repeated runtime-summary diagnostics during the 6-minute proof wait after the turn ended; remaining upload churn is proof-poll/runtime-save activity, not unbounded diagnostics segment publication. Next owner is CORE/response projection: host-native assistant rows must close the pending player ingress and project a hostContinue completion candidate instead of leaving the transaction with `responseCount: 0` and no CORE response ledger row.
- CORE mechanics checkpoint path-length closure, July 4: after the bounded checkpoint snapshot cut, the next one-user smoke exposed a different storage failure: `/api/files/upload` returned 500 for a CORE mechanics checkpoint whose file name was exactly 255 characters and whose Windows path was 315 characters. The failed upload was `...core-checkpoints-core-mechanics-outcome.chat-turn-ingress-...f2420ac6.v2.json`, not the larger save index, proving the root cause was checkpoint id/path growth from chat-derived outcome ids rather than payload size. `turn-commit-coordinator.mjs` now caps long CORE mechanics checkpoint ids at 72 characters with a stable 12-character hash suffix while preserving short readable ids. `smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs` now logs upload file names and base64 lengths for future storage triage without logging payload data. Focused proof passed for `node --check src/runtime/turn-commit-coordinator.mjs`, `test-turn-commit-coordinator-core-mechanics.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, and `node --check tools/scripts/smoke-sillytavern-live.mjs`. Served `directive-soak-b` `turn-commit-coordinator.mjs` was hash-synced (`AA514EE4C3E348D40A68C6AD7B173792668AEFBB8DC50B23CAE19BA6B0242FAE`), and one-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-short-core-checkpoint-id/report.json`: `ok: true`, generation timing `pass` from `coreStoreTurnTiming`/`coreProjection`, host-native completion `pass` from `coreStoreResponseLedger`/`coreProjection`, zero failed upload responses, and checkpoint upload file names reduced to 187 characters.
- Hot runtime save-index rewrite deferral, July 4: the post-checkpoint live proof still showed a save-bloat/hot-write problem: `directive-indexes-saves.v1.json` uploaded 40 times in one short single-user smoke, totaling about 16.4 MB. Runtime `markActive:false` persists were still calling `markCampaignSaveRuntimeV2State(...)`, rewriting the full global save index even after the save had stable v2 manifest authority. `campaign-start-controller.mjs` now defers save-index updates for hot `runtimePersist:*` writes only when the indexed save is already v2-owned and already carries bound chat/display metadata; first v1-to-v2 bridge writes, manual saves, autosaves, Save As, and metadata-establishing writes still update the index. `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` can skip the index write and return an explicit `indexWriteSkipped` entry, while `directive-storage-repository.mjs` treats stale indexed manifest hashes as advisory and falls back to the stable logical key while still enforcing the manifest's own self-hash. Focused proof passed for `node --check` on the changed storage/controller files, `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, `test-storage-cross-writer-v2.mjs`, `test-runtime-persist-coordinator.mjs`, and `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`. Served `directive-soak-b` storage/controller files were hash-synced, and one-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-hot-index-defer/report.json`: `ok: true`, CORE generation timing `pass`, CORE host-native completion `pass`, zero failed upload responses, and save-index uploads dropped from 40 to 3. Remaining hot-write owners are now CORE diagnostics tail churn (`core-diagnostics-0000-*`, 40 uploads and about 9.2 MB in the same run) and active materialized head rewrites (`head.v2.json`, 29 uploads and about 8.35 MB).
- CORE diagnostics bounded-segment cut, July 4: the next storage audit isolated `appendDiagnostics()` as another hot-write source. `commitV2DiagnosticsSegments(...)` was appending each new diagnostic entry to the existing diagnostics tail, and the overwrite-avoidance path then wrote a new hash-suffixed full-tail file. That made CORE diagnostics effectively quadratic during live turns: the preceding single-user smoke showed `core-diagnostics-0000-*` at 40 uploads, about 9.24 MB total, and a 462 KB max segment. `transaction-store-v2.mjs` now lets diagnostics commits append as new bounded segments instead of republishing the growing tail, while normal event/turn segment commits keep tail append behavior. A RED burst fixture in `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs` proved 12 repeated diagnostics appends previously collapsed into a growing tail and now produce 12 one-entry segment writes plus 12 manifest refs. Focused proof passed for `node --check src/storage/transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`. Served `directive-soak-b` `transaction-store-v2.mjs` was hash-synced (`4962A244AE25ACEDD7B90091A09BE7B830BE3C24086B71447411E9C5C799311F`), and one-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-diagnostics-bounded-segments/report.json`: `ok: true`, CORE generation timing `pass`, CORE host-native completion `pass`, zero failed upload responses, save-index stayed at 3 uploads, and CORE diagnostics no longer republished the full tail. The remaining dominant storage owner is active materialized head rewriting: `head.v2.json` still uploaded 32 times, totaling about 9.65 MB, so the next CORE storage slice should target materialized-head projection cadence/reuse rather than diagnostics.
- Active materialized-head projection-only reuse cut, July 4: a follow-up audit found `head.v2.json` rewrites were not only real state changes; derived runtime counters inside the active head (`runtimeSummary`, `runtimeHeadBudget`, `state.runtimeResume`, and `materializedHeadStateHash`) made diagnostics/model-call/sidecar projection-only changes look like full materialized-state changes. `transaction-store-v2.mjs` now reuses the existing active materialized-head ref when the only difference is volatile metadata or runtime projection-only counters, while still rewriting the head for actual compact campaign-state changes. `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` now recalculates/merges `runtimeResume` during v2 load so reused heads can still recover fresh model-call/sidecar counters from event/diagnostic segments, and `runtime-app.mjs` refreshes CORE projection evidence before explicit runtime-diagnostics persistence so the diagnostics segment, not the head, carries fresh sidecar/background evidence. Focused proof added a RED active-facade case where diagnostics-only CORE projection changes must not write `head.v2.json` and v2 load must derive the fresh resume count from segments; adjacent proof passed for `node --check` on the touched modules, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-runtime-persist-coordinator.mjs`, `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`, `test-storage-cross-writer-v2.mjs`, `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`. Served `directive-soak-b` `transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, and `runtime-app.mjs` were hash-synced, and one-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-head-reuse/report.json`: `ok: true`, CORE generation timing `pass`, host-native completion `pass`, zero failed upload responses, and active head uploads dropped to 13 uploads / about 2.95 MB in that shorter run. This is progress, not closure: the run stopped early on a scripted clarification, and `head.v2.json` remained the top Directive-owned byte source, so the next storage slice should reduce actual full-head persist cadence or move more active presentation roots behind segment/projection refs.
- Runtime active-head checkpoint reuse cut, July 4: after projection-only head reuse, the live proof still showed 25 tiny `checkpoints/runtime-active-head.v2.json` uploads in the same short run. Those writes were not the byte leader, but they were still unnecessary save churn: the checkpoint hash included runtime projection-only resume counters and the checkpoint writer always rewrote even when the semantic active head was unchanged. `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` now hashes the runtime-active-head checkpoint from the semantic head state with `runtimeResume` excluded, and `transaction-store-v2.mjs` reuses an equivalent existing checkpoint while ignoring volatile artifact metadata, `createdAt`, and checkpoint reason text. Focused proof added a RED active-facade assertion that diagnostics-only CORE projection changes must not rewrite the runtime-active-head checkpoint; adjacent proof passed for `node --check` on the touched modules, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`, and `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`. Served `directive-soak-b` storage files were hash-synced, and one-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-checkpoint-reuse/report.json`: `ok: true`, CORE generation timing `pass`, host-native completion `pass`, zero failed upload responses, and runtime-active-head checkpoint uploads dropped from 25 to 13. Active `head.v2.json` stayed at 13 uploads / about 2.94 MB, so the next high-value cut remains full-head cadence/root splitting rather than checkpoint metadata.
- Active materialized-head root split, July 4: the active save head no longer stores the full compact campaign state inline. `head.v2.json` is now a compact root-ref index with `stateStorage: "rootRefs"` and one reusable `head-roots/{root}.v2.json` artifact per top-level campaign-state root; `runtimeResume` stays in projection-only head metadata so diagnostics-only CORE projection changes do not rotate a semantic root ref. `loadV2MaterializedHead(...)` hydrates root refs back into the legacy caller contract while still verifying the manifest head ref when one is provided, and active-save/repository restore paths now use that verified hydrated loader. Focused proof added a RED transaction-store assertion that raw `head.v2.json` must not contain inline `state`, must point at the large continuity root, must hydrate the continuity dossier, and must not rewrite an unchanged continuity root when only another root changes. Adjacent proof passed for `node --check` on `logical-storage-paths.mjs`, `transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, and `directive-storage-repository.mjs`; `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`; `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`; `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`; `test-old-save-importer-v2.mjs`; `test-storage-cross-writer-v2.mjs`; `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`; and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`. The 5000-message scale harness now reports `materializedHeadBytes: 22081` versus a `legacyFullSaveBytes` fixture of `16177360`, with the large active state shifted into reusable roots and auxiliary artifacts. Served `directive-soak-b` storage files were hash-synced, and one-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-root-split-head/2026-07-04T13-02-42-560Z/report.json`: `ok: true`, one-turn warning only, served-extension freshness `pass`, CORE generation start max latency `2291 ms`, host-native completion max latency `82092 ms`, no failed model calls, and story/factual transcript audits passed. The live save `campaign-1783170183014-2-5e08a0ee` / `save-1783170183014-3-a865b56a` had raw active `head.v2.json` at 13,939 filesystem bytes with no inline `state`, 34 active head roots totaling 212,517 bytes, and root reuse visible from mostly activation-time root mtimes. Remaining storage work should focus on residual diagnostics segment count/cadence and any oversized roots such as continuity, quest, and thread ledgers, not on monolithic head rewrites.
- Replacement diagnostics cleanup, July 4: active full-state persists still wrote a fresh fingerprinted `diagnostics/0000-*.v2.json` replacement whenever compact runtime diagnostics changed, which preserved crash safety but left obsolete diagnostics files behind after the new save/campaign manifest was published. `transaction-store-v2.mjs` now keeps the versioned write-before-manifest safety rule, then best-effort deletes superseded diagnostics refs only after the replacement manifest and campaign manifest are written and verified. The cleanup is scoped to replaced diagnostics refs for the same campaign/save/layout and is non-fatal if the host lacks delete support. Focused proof added a RED transaction-store assertion that changed replacement diagnostics delete the superseded segment and do not accumulate orphaned diagnostics files; adjacent proof passed for `node --check src/storage/transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-storage-cross-writer-v2.mjs`, `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, and `test-old-save-importer-v2.mjs`. Served `directive-soak-b` `transaction-store-v2.mjs` was hash-synced, and one-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-diagnostics-cleanup/2026-07-04T13-13-45-787Z/report.json`: `ok: true`, one-turn warning only, served-extension freshness `pass`, generation start max latency `2345 ms`, host-native completion max latency `123985 ms`, no failed model calls, and story/factual transcript audits passed. The live save `campaign-1783170846387-2-be512baf` / `save-1783170846387-3-7a3aa812` had exactly one active diagnostics file, exactly one active diagnostics manifest ref, and zero unreferenced active diagnostics files; CORE diagnostics remained four referenced append-only segments with zero unreferenced files. Remaining storage work shifts from orphan cleanup to true CORE diagnostics cadence: batching/summarizing CORE diagnostic appends where safe without losing recovery/proof evidence.

- FORGE accepted-batch settlement diagnostic coalescing, July 4: `settleAcceptedBatch(...)` now accepts the same caller-owned diagnostic sink pattern used by `runProviderBatch(...)`, but only for final non-recovery `settled` / `noChange` evidence. Stale, rejected, hash-mismatch, replay-mismatch, and missing-writer cases still use FORGE's immediate diagnostic append/failure-warning path so rejection evidence is not lost behind an async batch. `campaign-sidecar-scheduler.mjs` passes the sink only when it has `appendCoreDiagnostic` or `appendCoreDiagnosticsBatch`, causing normal accepted sidecar settlement diagnostics to join the active scheduler batch instead of creating an extra one-entry CORE diagnostics segment. - SillyTavern settings bloat audit, July 4: the active `directive-soak-b` `settings.json` measured about `1088330` bytes after the provider switch, but the bloat is not Directive campaign data in SillyTavern settings. Top-level `extension_settings` accounted for about `700703` bytes; its largest entries were `rpg_tracker` (`373486` bytes, including `routerHistory` and chat memo snapshots), `wandlight` (`113607` bytes, mostly `loredeckLibrary` packs), `vectfox` (`22333` bytes), and `connectionManager` (`17103` bytes). `extension_settings.directive` was only `466` bytes with `providers`, `enabled`, and `presetAutoCheckDismissedVersion`. This means the current Directive save-file contract remains intact for the inspected user: campaign runtime/state is not being parked in `settings.json`. Continue treating Directive save growth as a CORE/v2 artifact and hot-save cadence problem, while treating ST global settings bloat as external compatibility/host-environment evidence rather than a Directive storage-contract violation.

The live `save-1783193490432-3-8f5c3463` prompt diagnostics segment had two promptSchedule entries and was `85355` bytes on disk; `promptBudgetBlocked` embedded a full `promptBudgetTrace` of about `39.5KB`, mostly incidental active-cast included/omitted refs even though the real block was `protectedContinuity`. `lens-prompt-scheduler.mjs` now keeps the full `promptBudgetTrace` on the in-memory flush result and packet, but durable CORE prompt diagnostics persist only `promptBudgetTraceRef`, compact `promptBudgetTraceSummary` lane statuses/counts/blocking ids, compact external-target status summaries, prompt-key counts/hashes, and final schedule status. The scheduler also folds external environment observation into the final `installed` / `promptBudgetBlocked` evidence instead of writing a standalone `externalEnvironmentObserved` row for the same flush, and it strips diagnostic-only `rawPromptBody`, `rawResponse`, `vectorPayload`, `apiKey`, and `cacheRecord` fields before redaction/persistence. A read-only explorer identified the next macro blocker as remaining production authority in non-terminal `runtimeTracking.pendingInteractions` plus terminal decision mirror/read paths; that blocker was handled in the following pending-interaction read-authority slice.
- Pending-interaction read-authority retirement, July 4: runtime read paths no longer fall back to old `runtimeTracking.pendingInteractions`. `runtime-app.pendingInteractionProjectionRows(...)` and `chat-turn-orchestrator.pendingInteractionRows(...)` now read non-terminal pauses only from CORE read projections, REPAIR rollback restore keeps the old pending array empty, and terminal checkpoint decisions are resolved only through `terminalDecisionLedgerView(...)`. `isPendingInteractionProjectionRow(...)` no longer accepts `terminalDecisionProjection`, and the dead legacy pending helper residue was removed from `state-delta-gateway.mjs`, so terminal decisions cannot re-enter the non-terminal pending surface through compatibility mirrors. Focused proof passed for syntax checks on the touched runtime and test files plus `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-terminal-outcome.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, `test-runtime-persist-coordinator.mjs`, `test-campaign-end-condition-service.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; the full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed `202` checks, and the 5000-message gate stayed at `materializedHeadBytes: 22081`, `hotTurnWrites: 4`, `hotTurnReads: 8`, and `legacyFullSaveBytes: 16177360`. Served `directive-soak-b` runtime files were hash-synced for `runtime-app.mjs` (`71910AF34527DF0F10B53F1134EEB7FF2D5E6C284DEE056C29A2FE011E6D40C0`), `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` (`355A1360E912A6D35A3864A07275B2A50E43FB28B3227DEB1DE39823287DBE9C`), `repair-command-boundary.mjs` (`8A90D4B660A27DD58576B00A23002BC59CF6D955FA12DC6EE7367E7E9ED0A6CC`), `campaign-end-condition-service.mjs` (`1A518C99ED35073C67A7E3C9B31F2B45E1698C2B75058E8DB61EA137206DA07F`), and `state-delta-gateway.mjs` (`C231F9A0838BABA0D9F83A14287FA6D0383DD875597CFF77E71710C5ED4B3A4C`). The requested single-user provider profile was active for `directive-soak-b`: host selected profile plus Directive Utility and Reasoning lanes all point to `bad053d6-6fbc-44b6-967e-1451edd4ab61`, `nanogpt deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro-cheaper:thinking - Provider`; the final `settings.json` had no BOM, no unused upper-case `Directive` settings key, and measured `1105453` bytes after SillyTavern rewrote it. One-user Playwright smoke passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-pending-interaction-read-authority-rerun/report-summary.json`: `ok: true`, live generation enabled, user `directive-soak-b`, campaign `campaign-1783204407338-2-66911414`, save `save-1783204407338-3-244bc658`, chat `Directive - Ashes of Peace (186) - 2026-07-04@16h33m28s721ms`, `sentMessageCount: 3`, zero failed model calls, `sidecarCount: 11`, zero pending interactions, CORE host-native completion proof `pass` with two completions and `hostNativeCompletionMaxLatencyMs: 131650`, and warning-quality generation timing because this path did not start a Directive model call. Direct save inspection stayed compact with v1 save shell `111734` bytes, `111` save-scoped files totaling `1312008` bytes, and old `runtimeTracking.history`, `ingressLedger`, `responseLedger`, `recoveryJournal`, `sidecarJournal`, `modelCallJournal`, `pendingInteractions`, and terminal decisions all `0`. Treat this as deterministic read-authority proof plus served-runtime/save-contract regression evidence, not as pending-pause-path live certification.
- CORE-only runtime overlay cut, July 4: `runtime-ledger-view` no longer overlays old ingress/response rows once CORE projections for that family exist, even when callers request `legacyFallback` or `runtimeOverlay`. Narrow hot-row exceptions are now explicit and local: runtime-app model-call targeting, post-commit/advisory guard lookup, turn-commit mechanics checkpointing, sidecar source snapshotting, response-dispatcher host-observation claims, and provider-fallback retry targeting may read raw `runtimeTracking` only for tagged CORE projection claims or in-flight source identity needed to close a CORE-owned operation. Response retry now allows an authorized `directive.repairResponseRetryActuationDecision.v1` to bypass an open CORE recovery projection so the missing visible response can be posted and the recovery can close, instead of treating `responseRetryRequired` as an already-posted duplicate. CORE projections now carry the fields those paths need (`coreTransactionId`, `ingressId`, source frame ids, strategy, release timing, and recovery response rows), and hot sidecar/model-call diagnostics attach to CORE transactions without restoring old-ledger authority. Focused proof passed for `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-campaign-sidecar-scheduler.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-chat-response-recovery.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`. Full `node tools\scripts\run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed `202` checks after the cut, including storage scale 5000 with `materializedHeadBytes: 22081`, `hotTurnWrites: 4`, `hotTurnReads: 8`, and `legacyFullSaveBytes: 16177360`. The served `directive-soak-b` extension was synced from the repo and the SillyTavern Connection Manager plus Directive utility/reasoning lanes were verified on `nanogpt deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro-cheaper:thinking - Provider`. Live single-user smoke evidence exists in `artifacts/live-smoke/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-04-runtime-overlay-core-only-one-turn`: CORE persisted one player ingress, host-native continuation timing from CORE projection, and `generationStartLatencyMs: 1416`; the visible host response completed after `105752` ms. The harness still marked the run failed because its success predicate did not count the host-native continuation in the final model-call/Directive-owned-response growth check, so this slice is deterministically green and live-evidenced, but not yet live-certified by the current Playwright report.
- V2 storage fail-closed cut, July 4: generic save load and active recovery no longer silently revive stale v1 checkpoint payloads for saves whose index entry or payload marks v2 as the runtime authority. `loadActiveCampaignStateV2(...)` now returns `campaignState: null` on v2 read failure instead of exposing `fallbackCampaignState`; `loadCampaignSaveFromStorage(...)` throws `DIRECTIVE_V2_SAVE_STATE_UNAVAILABLE` for broken runtime-v2 bridge entries before raw `payload.campaignState` fallback; and `recoverActiveCampaignSave(...)` records `active-save-v2-state-unavailable`, skips the broken v2-owned candidate, and may recover a different valid save instead of downgrading the broken save to its stale v1 checkpoint. Plain v1 campaign saves and explicit State Safety/checkpoint flows remain separate. Focused proof passed for `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-storage-cross-writer-v2.mjs`, `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, and `test-old-save-importer-v2.mjs`, plus syntax checks on touched storage/test files. Follow-up Wave 1 debt remains: partial CORE turn/replacement history and legacy import turn rows are still import/checkpoint evidence and must not become replay authority without CORE-owned projection evidence.
- CORE turn/replacement replay authority cut, July 4: active-save v2 and runtime merge now treat CORE turn-ledger projections as the only active replay authority for `turnLedger.entries`, `replacementHistory`, and `lastCommittedOutcomeId`. Old `campaignState.turnLedger.entries` and `campaignState.turnLedger.replacementHistory` no longer provide active checkpoint refs, transaction ids, retained-snapshot flags, or unmatched replay rows; legacy turn data remains migration/checkpoint evidence outside the active turn projection. Old-save import no longer writes legacy `turnLedger.entries` into v2 `turnSegments`; `legacyTurnImported` events and legacy import/checkpoint artifacts remain migration and State Safety evidence only. Review-agent follow-up also constrained transient raw ingress/response hot-row lookups: exact in-flight ids may still attach local source evidence, but raw `runtimeTracking` rows can no longer win by broad `outcomeId`, `turnId`, `hostMessageId`, or `ingressId` matches after CORE projection reads. CORE mechanics commits now write `snapshotBeforeRetained` into the CORE turn record when the pre-outcome checkpoint is created, so rerun/delete authorization no longer borrows that flag from the old turn mirror. Focused proof passed for syntax checks on `runtime-app.mjs`, `turn-commit-coordinator.mjs`, `active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `transaction-store-v2.mjs`, and touched tests; deterministic gates passed for `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-old-save-importer-v2.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-turn-commit-coordinator-core-mechanics.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-runtime-stage18-rerun-branch-recovery.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, and `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`. No Playwright live soak was run for this slice; treat it as deterministic Wave 1 authority proof, with live certification deferred to the later integration/certification pass.
- Scene Reconciliation/Scene Handshake owner-root cut, July 4: `sceneReconciliation` and `sceneHandshake` are now top-level mutable owner ledgers, while nested `runtimeTracking.sceneReconciliation` and `runtimeTracking.sceneHandshake` are reset to empty compatibility shells during initialization and snapshots. `initializeCampaignRuntimeTracking(...)` no longer promotes tagged nested scene rows into top-level owner state; only valid top-level SRE owner rows and explicitly tagged Scene Handshake telemetry rows survive. Scene Handshake writes now target `sceneHandshake.*`, its result-only commit path allows only that owner root, and REPAIR/message recovery/idempotency helpers read top-level `sceneHandshake` instead of nested runtime tracking fallback. Dependent invalidation tests now seed top-level Scene Handshake rows, the live Scene Handshake smoke helper inspects/export-checks top-level `sceneHandshake` and asserts the exported nested root remains an empty shell, and the architecture skeleton guards against reintroducing nested handshake fallback. - Scene Handshake latest-pair authority split, July 4: `sceneHandshakeLedgerRecord(...)` now stamps `sreSceneHandshakeProjection` / `sourceSettlementLatestPair` / `directive.sceneHandshakeLedgerProjectionRef.v1` only for latest-pair SRE owner records. Legacy `sceneHandshakeSettler` accepted/fallback/defer/internal-review rows now persist only as `legacySceneHandshakeSettlerTelemetry` with `projectionSource: "sceneHandshakeSettler"` and `directive.sceneHandshakeSettlerTelemetry.v1`, so legacy materialization cannot certify SRE ownership. `normalizedSceneHandshakeLedger(...)` preserves valid top-level SRE rows and valid legacy telemetry rows while still dropping untagged or forged rows, and nested runtimeTracking scene rows remain ignored. Live certification policy now treats `sourceSettlementLatestPair` as the owner role and `sceneHandshakeSettler` as materialization telemetry; `smoke-scene-handshake-live.mjs` reports both role families and fails SRE owner certification if a legacy row claims owner authority. - Latest-pair Source Settlement contract cut, July 4: `source-settlement-latest-pair-contract.mjs` now owns the `sourceSettlementLatestPair` role id, `sre/latestPair` owner-mode tuple, `sreSceneHandshakeProjection` authority, and `directive.sceneHandshakeLedgerProjectionRef.v1` compatibility mirror shape. `scene-handshake-settler.mjs` now imports that contract for latest-pair ledger authority and SRE metadata instead of defining those identity strings locally, while legacy `sceneHandshakeSettler` rows still stay legacy telemetry. This is a prerequisite cut, not the full native extraction: the latest-pair provider/apply body still lives behind Scene Handshake helpers, and the next macro refactor should extract a non-circular Scene Handshake source adapter before moving `createLatestPairSreSettlementProvider(...)`, frame/source composition, terminal latest-pair apply, and strict `settleLatestPairSource(...)` into the latest-pair owner module. - Latest-pair owner orchestration cut, July 4: `source-settlement-latest-pair-owner.mjs` now owns the terminal latest-pair SRE service composition, source-frame/source projection, apply-owner guard, CORE diagnostic decision path, Scene Handshake ledger apply operation, allowed-root list, committed-root/prompt-dirty interpretation, and accepted time-boundary handoff for the Scene Handshake latest-pair source. `scene-handshake-settler.mjs` now delegates `runTerminalLatestPairSourceSettlement(...)` into that owner and passes only domain hooks: selected-variant normalization, Scene Handshake ledger record creation, ledger operation projection, and time-advance commit. `source-settlement-latest-pair.mjs` no longer statically imports `scene-handshake-settler.mjs`; its public facade owns the latest-pair boundary and isolates the remaining provider/prompt/validation coupling behind an explicit lazy `sceneHandshakeMigrationAdapter`. At this point in the cut sequence, prompt construction, response parsing, and validation still came from the Scene Handshake migration adapter; the provider extraction immediately below supersedes the prompt/parse part of that debt. Live freshness now checks `source-settlement-latest-pair-contract.mjs` and `source-settlement-latest-pair-owner.mjs` in `smoke-scene-handshake-live.mjs`, and the skeleton test now fails if the public facade reintroduces a static Scene Handshake import or if Scene Handshake retakes SRE service/frame/source orchestration. - Latest-pair provider extraction cut, July 4: `source-settlement-latest-pair-provider.mjs` now owns latest-pair prompt construction, structured JSON parse, dedicated `sourceSettlementLatestPair` role call, 8-second timeout, provider error normalization, sanitized failure codes, and validator-hook orchestration. `scene-handshake-settler.mjs` keeps the Scene Handshake validation helper for this cut, but `createLatestPairSreSettlementProvider(...)` delegates provider execution to the latest-pair provider runtime and supplies only `validateSceneHandshakeSettlement` as a domain hook. The public `source-settlement-latest-pair.mjs` facade now uses the native provider runtime directly and keeps the lazy Scene Handshake migration adapter limited to validation and strict legacy settlement fallback. This reduces the previous circular owner shape: Scene Handshake no longer owns provider prompt/timeout/model-call orchestration. The validation portion of the migration debt is superseded by the validation extraction cut immediately below; remaining latest-pair debt is the strict Scene Handshake materialization adapter/fallback. Live freshness now also checks `source-settlement-latest-pair-provider.mjs`, and the skeleton contract fails if provider prompt/timeout strings move back into Scene Handshake or if the facade regains a static Scene Handshake import.
