- Production latest-pair provider gate reopened, July 4: the post-send browser-freeze mitigation had left `runtime-app.mjs` wiring `enableDefaultLatestPairSettlementProvider: false`, which made the Scene Handshake live proof structurally impossible after the native SRE latest-pair adapter landed. The one-user `directive-soak-b` `smoke-scene-handshake-live.mjs` run created campaign `campaign-1783216050979-2-8417986b`, save `save-1783216050979-3-dee7debf`, and chat `Directive - Ashes of Peace (189) - 2026-07-04@19h47m32s276ms`; served freshness passed and the visible Directive response posted, but the harness timed out after 240 seconds waiting for `sceneHandshake.lastResult` because no `sceneHandshake` state root could be written while the provider was disabled. Direct evidence showed `runtimeSummary.ingressCount: 1`, `responseCount: 1`, `recoveryCount: 1`, no `sceneHandshake` root, CORE events `turnObserved -> phaseAdvanced -> mechanicsCommitted -> recoveryRequired`, and a Utility classifier model-call failure after `32226 ms` with sanitized code `provider_reasoning_only`. The host selected profile plus Directive Utility and Reasoning lanes were already on `bad053d6-6fbc-44b6-967e-1451edd4ab61`, `nanogpt deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro-cheaper:thinking - Provider`; no settings write was needed. The runtime wiring is now flipped back to `enableDefaultLatestPairSettlementProvider: true`, and skeleton/live-prep guards require the production SRE latest-pair provider to be enabled. Focused deterministic proof passed for `node --check src/runtime/runtime-app.mjs`, `node --check tools/scripts/test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `node --check tools/scripts/test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, and `git diff --check` with only CRLF warnings. Served public ST files were hash-synced for the touched runtime/SRE modules. The post-flip one-user Scene Handshake smoke then failed earlier at Utility provider precheck: ST/Directive still reported the requested NanoGPT profile as selected and ready for both lanes, but the actual precheck request returned `DIRECTIVE_PROVIDER_TEST_FAILED` / `API request failed`. This is now a provider/profile availability blocker, not a disabled Scene Handshake architecture blocker. The earlier `provider_reasoning_only` classifier failure remains separate provider-profile evidence to watch once the API request succeeds.
- External-context timing contract cut, July 5: delegated smoke external-context summaries no longer report `status: "pass"` when observable context-extension targets lack timing attribution. `soak-sillytavern-campaign-live.mjs` now derives `timingRequiredTargets` from host-observed target state, treats disabled/unobservable targets as `targetsTimingNotRequired`, and downgrades active/observable missing timing to `warning`; full certification summarization still fails the artifact if any timing-required target is missing attribution, even if a stale artifact claims `status: "pass"`. Focused proof passed for `test-external-context-summary-artifact.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-external-context-observer.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-external-context-readiness-preflight.mjs`, `test-continuity-matrix-five-user-soak-coordinator.mjs`, `test-continuity-matrix-full-certification-preflight.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). One-user Playwright/SillyTavern proof passed at `artifacts/live-soak/sillytavern-campaign/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-05-external-context-timing-contract/report.json` with overall `warning` only because the run was intentionally limited to one turn; CORE generation-start timing passed from `coreStoreTurnTiming` with max latency `1422 ms`, CORE host-native completion passed from `coreStoreResponseLedger`/`coreProjection` with one completion and max completion latency `14132 ms`, and `host-extensions/external-context-summary.json` now correctly reports `status: "warning"`, `timingRequiredTargets: ["memoryBooks","summaryception","vectFox"]`, `targetsTimingNotRequired: ["stLorebooks"]`, and missing timing for the three active/observable targets. This keeps external-context evidence honest without importing Memory Books, Summaryception, or VectFox content as Directive authority.
- External-context bounded timing and VectFox network attribution cut, July 5: active Memory Books and Summaryception targets that expose useful range/staleness evidence but no timing API now emit bounded `extension-timing-not-exposed` diagnostics instead of silent missing timing, while VectFox retrieval latency is attributed only from observed Playwright browser-network request/response pairs for `/api/vector/query-multi`. Summary coverage now separates `targetsWithTiming`, `targetsWithBoundedTimingUnavailable`, and `targetsMissingTiming`; real timing wins over bounded-unavailable, and certification still fails only on silent missing timing or unsafe/malformed artifacts. No private extension internals or raw generated memory/summary/vector payloads become Directive state. Focused proof passed for `node --check` on the touched soak/coordinator scripts, `test-external-prompt-environment.mjs`, `test-external-context-summary-artifact.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-external-context-observer.mjs`, `test-sillytavern-external-context-readiness-preflight.mjs`, `test-continuity-matrix-five-user-soak-coordinator.mjs`, `test-continuity-matrix-full-certification-preflight.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). Served `directive-soak-b` `architecture-redesign-contracts.mjs` was hash-synced, and one-user Playwright/SillyTavern proof passed at `artifacts/live-soak/sillytavern-campaign/architecture-redesign-single-user-2026-07-05-external-context-vectfox-network-timing-clean/report.json`: served-extension freshness and extension-sync gates passed; delegated smoke completed one planned turn; CORE generation-start timing passed with max latency `1495 ms`; CORE host-native completion passed with max completion latency `26856 ms`; external-context timing coverage reported `status: "limited"`, `targetsWithTiming: ["vectFox"]`, `targetsWithBoundedTimingUnavailable: ["memoryBooks","summaryception"]`, `targetsMissingTiming: []`, and VectFox `retrievalLatencyMs: 2979` from `timingSource: "browser-network"`. The report remains `warning` only because the run intentionally used `DIRECTIVE_SOAK_TURN_LIMIT=1`, not because of external-context evidence loss.
- Scene Handshake latest-pair live revalidation, July 5: the provider-gate blocker from July 4 is no longer current. A one-user `directive-soak-b` `smoke-scene-handshake-live.mjs` run completed and printed `ok: true` in `artifacts/live-smoke/scene-handshake-latest-pair-provider-rerun-2026-07-05.log`: served extension freshness passed, Utility provider precheck passed, one Scene Handshake settlement committed with `authority: "sreSceneHandshakeProjection"`, `projectionSource: "sourceSettlementLatestPair"`, and `ownerCertified: true`; `sceneHandshakeModelCalls` and `sourceSettlementModelCalls` were both empty in the artifact because the live path used the SRE latest-pair projection owner; duplicate reobserve, reopen, Save Game As branch, wrong-chat isolation, and source-edit invalidation all passed, with edited source status `invalidated`. The PowerShell command wrapper reported a nonzero NativeCommandError because the smoke writes progress to stderr under `*>` redirection, but the parsed artifact is the proof result. A later clean-output rerun timed out after campaign setup and is discarded as inconclusive harness/runtime-latency evidence, not a failed Scene Handshake owner proof. This closes the specific disabled-provider / owner-root live blocker; full Wave 4 certification remains separate and must still validate finished paths instead of reopening redesign scope.
- Scene Handshake legacy telemetry authority retirement, July 5: `sceneHandshake` ledgers now accept only `sreSceneHandshakeProjection` rows with `projectionSource: "sourceSettlementLatestPair"` and `directive.sceneHandshakeLedgerProjectionRef.v1`; `legacySceneHandshakeSettlerTelemetry` is no longer preserved as durable Scene Handshake ledger authority. The older direct Scene Handshake wrapper and the latest-pair scene adapter both stamp records through `latestPairSourceSettlementAuthority(...)` and always attach `latestPairSourceSettlementMetadata(...)`, so deterministic fallback, provider failure, parse failure, rejection, and accepted-settlement paths record the same SRE/latest-pair owner contract. Live soak policy no longer requires `sceneHandshakeSettler` materialization telemetry and instead requires absence of legacy materialization evidence. Focused proof passed for syntax on `state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, `source-settlement-latest-pair-scene-adapter.mjs`, `smoke-scene-handshake-live.mjs`, `soak-sillytavern-campaign-live.mjs`, and touched tests; deterministic proof passed for `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-scene-handshake-settler.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, and `test-source-reconciliation-engine-synthetic.mjs`. This closes the stale compatibility-test pressure that still blessed legacy Scene Handshake telemetry after the July 5 latest-pair live proof.
- CORE/v2 save authority cut, July 5: raw `directive.campaignSave.payload.campaignState` is no longer a runtime load/recovery authority. `loadCampaignSaveFromStorage(...)` now requires a v2 manifest/runtime bridge and throws `DIRECTIVE_V2_SAVE_REQUIRED` for unconverted v1 payloads; `recoverActiveCampaignSave(...)` skips raw v1 candidates with `active-save-v2-manifest-required`; first save, manual save, Save As, and autosave immediately persist v2 runtime authority after any checkpoint shell write. State Safety remains the explicit checkpoint lane, but active runtime reads, save verification, autosave fixtures, and manual-save controller assertions now use CORE/v2 authority. Stale compatibility assertions in `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs` and `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs` were converted instead of making new code satisfy old v1 ledgers. Focused proof passed for `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, `test-campaign-start-and-save.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-old-save-importer-v2.mjs`, `test-runtime-persist-coordinator.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-runtime-shell-creator-flow.mjs`, `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, and `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`; full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed `201` checks. The 5000-message gate stayed compact at `materializedHeadBytes: 22081`, `hotTurnWrites: 4`, `hotTurnReads: 8`, and `legacyFullSaveBytes: 16177360`. One-user Playwright proof passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/scene-handshake-core-v2-save-authority-2026-07-05.log`: served freshness `ok: true`, mismatch count `0`, SRE latest-pair settlement `authority: "sreSceneHandshakeProjection"`, `projectionSource: "sourceSettlementLatestPair"`, `ownerCertified: true`, no Scene Handshake or source-settlement model-call rows, duplicate reobserve/reopen/Save Game As/wrong-chat/source-edit invalidation all `ok: true`, and live save-index inspection confirmed original save `runtimeStorageFormat: "v2"` with `v2ManifestRef` plus Save As branch `storageFormat: "v2"` / `payloadKind: "directive.saveManifest.v2"`.
- Old-save importer retirement, July 5: `importCampaignSaveRecordToV2(...)`, its legacy importer helpers, and `tools/scripts/test-old-save-importer-v2.mjs` were removed from the active redesign. `transaction-store-v2.mjs` no longer carries old-save compatibility migration helpers, no longer emits legacy import event/diagnostic/checkpoint artifacts, and the skeleton guard now fails if that lane is reintroduced. Old v1 payloads are handled by the CORE/v2 save-required path instead of a compatibility importer. Focused proof passed for syntax on `transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, and `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`; deterministic proof passed for `test-transaction-store-v2.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, and `test-runtime-campaign-start-controller.mjs`; full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` passed `201` checks. A scoped source search found no importer/export/helper strings outside the skeleton guard canaries. Served `directive-soak-b` `transaction-store-v2.mjs` was hash-synced (`4281C2BF3E82179FD33333E56901C88057C5170BDD50FAA34D779D396E1DFD75`), and one-user Playwright proof passed at `artifacts/live-smoke/scene-handshake-old-save-importer-retired-2026-07-05.log`: served freshness passed, fresh Ashes save `save-1783253867913-3-38b06277` exported, Save Game As branch `save-1783253998985-4-bf4601c6` succeeded, duplicate reobserve/reopen/wrong-chat/source-edit invalidation all passed.
- REPAIR projection contract cut, July 5: source-mutation and response-recovery policy evidence now uses REPAIR-owned `repairProjection` / `directive.repairProjection.v2` instead of the stale `legacyProjection` field and `directive.repairLegacyProjection.v1` kind. `repair-runtime.mjs` constructs the REPAIR projection, `message-reconciler.mjs` consumes it for bridge status/action mirroring and rollback authorization, `core-store-v2.mjs` persists rollback-actuation projection evidence under the same REPAIR-owned field, and runtime/live mutation reports now summarize `repairProjection`. The skeleton guard now rejects reintroducing the stale field or schema kind in REPAIR source-mutation/response-recovery policy. This is an implementation cut inside the REPAIR/SRE authority exit, not a Wave 4 certification claim. Focused proof passed for syntax on `repair-runtime.mjs`, `message-reconciler.mjs`, `core-store-v2.mjs`, `runtime-app.mjs`, focused tests, and the message edit/delete live runners; deterministic proof passed for `test-repair-runtime.mjs`, `test-message-reconciler-source-mutation.mjs`, `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-chat-response-recovery.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, and `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`. No one-user Playwright run was started for this contract-only slice; live proof remains an integration/certification gate after the REPAIR path is served and bundled with adjacent runtime changes.
- CORE response projection envelope cut, July 5: response read-projection authority now uses the CORE-owned `responses` collection in `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections` instead of persisting response rows under the old-ledger-shaped `responseLedger` key. `runtime-ledger-view.mjs` still exposes `responseLedger` as a derived runtime view for existing callers, but `state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `directive-storage-repository.mjs`, and runtime persist merge paths now write/load/merge the `responses` authority key and strip the old alias from persisted envelopes. This removes another direct old-ledger-shaped durable response surface while keeping the hot runtime API stable. Focused proof passed for syntax on the touched runtime/storage modules; deterministic proof passed for `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`, `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs`, `test-current-chat-campaign-scope.mjs`, `test-runtime-persist-coordinator.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, and `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`. One-user served-copy proof is required before treating this as live-certified CORE/v2 response authority.
- Active-head idempotence and lifecycle v2 evidence cut, July 5: `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` now recompacts already-compact Command Log heads without dropping `summaryInputCount`, `summaryInputsHash`, or assisted-summary refs, so admin prompt rebuilds such as campaign difficulty changes cannot appear to rewrite Command Log evidence. Compact administrative lifecycle records now persist through v2 event segments as `runtimeLifecycleProjected` evidence and reload into bounded `runtimeTracking.lifecycleJournal` instead of relying on old recovery rows or raw runtimeTracking authority. Focused proof passed for `test-active-save-facade-v2.mjs` and `test-campaign-difficulty-runtime.mjs`; adjacent proof passed for `test-runtime-persist-coordinator.mjs`, `test-state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-storage-cross-writer-v2.mjs`, and `test-directive-storage-repository.mjs`. One-user Playwright proof was attempted at the availability gate, but SillyTavern was not reachable on `127.0.0.1:8000` (`TcpTestSucceeded: false`), so Wave 4 live certification remains the only current blocker rather than a new implementation red.

The successful run is `status: "warning"` only because `DIRECTIVE_SOAK_TURN_LIMIT=1` intentionally limited the 52-turn script; served-extension freshness, extension-sync barrier, delegated chat-native smoke, model-call failure policy, story-quality transcript review, generation-start timing, host-native completion, and factual-grounding audit all passed. Recorded timing: generation start max latency `1577 ms`; terminal host-native completion max latency `30959 ms`, sourced from persisted CORE projections. This evidence validates the current finished path without certifying the full redesign: implementation completion still means frozen exits 1-4 are done, while Wave 4 certification remains a later full-depth proof gate.
- Full one-user Wave 4 rehearsal accounting fix, July 5: the uncapped `directive-soak-b` Playwright/SillyTavern run at `artifacts/live-soak/sillytavern-campaign/2026-07-05T15-08-56-397Z/report.json` executed the full 52-script Ashes path with served-extension freshness, extension-sync barrier, no failed model calls, CORE generation-start timing pass, CORE host-native completion pass, and factual-grounding pass. The raw report initially failed `live-smoke-52-turn-delegation` because the parent soak assessment compared `sentMessageCount: 50` to all `52` script records. That was a proof-harness accounting bug: `soak-turn-11` (`briefOnly`) and `soak-turn-13` (`cancel`) are Assist-only script records that intentionally do not send a player chat message, while the live log still contains turn-start/turn-end records and fact checks through `soak-turn-52`. `liveSmokeDelegationAssessment(...)` now counts expected player sends from the same Assist semantics as the smoke runner, so the artifact replays as `status: "warning"` with `plannedTurns: 52`, `expectedSendTurns: 50`, and `sentTurns: 50` instead of a completion failure. Focused proof passed for `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, the artifact replay, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). Remaining Wave 4 status is not live-certified yet: the strict report still has a story-quality warning and external-context timing coverage is `limited` (`VectFox` timed; Memory Books and Summaryception bounded timing unavailable), so final certification still needs a clean release judgment pass instead of expanding redesign scope.
- Wave 4 story-quality hardening, July 5: the first full one-user rehearsal exposed proof-quality issues rather than a new architecture owner: turn 1 could devolve into a too-thin `**` host-visible assistant row, and the retcon/reconciliation script around turns 29-34 still contained meta-style player prompts that invited generic fallback prose. The Ashes script now uses concrete in-world command prose for the first bridge handoff and for the recent/deep retcon, reconciliation, recalculation, and branch-only turns; the live harness preflight rejects the old `continues the scene in third person` and `continuity stress case` meta prompts. The local routine fallback in `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` now responds to visible correction/withdrawal/recalculation text with specific bridge-status narration instead of the generic "order is acknowledged" paragraph. Focused proof passed for `test-live-soak-prep.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-story-quality-review-preflight.mjs`, and syntax checks for the touched runtime/live-soak files. The bounded Playwright smoke at `artifacts/live-soak/sillytavern-campaign/2026-07-05T17-21-15-083Z/report.json` passed served-extension freshness, extension sync, no failed model calls, deterministic and model-assisted story-quality review, generation-start timing, host-native completion, and factual grounding; the first Directive response is substantive rather than `**`. The report still remains `warning` because it is limited to two turns and the delegated smoke summary carries broader quality warning status from early sidecar rejection delta, so it is bounded Wave 4 evidence for the finished path, not live certification and not permission to reopen the redesign definition.

- FORGE/CORE sidecar stale-revision cut, July 5: regular campaign sidecar accepted batches now preserve an explicit `baseMechanicsRevision: null` as "source-bound background settlement; no global mechanics gate" instead of falling back to `baseRevisions.mechanics` or converting null to stale revision `0`. CORE still enforces numeric `baseMechanicsRevision` when supplied, while null/undefined is omitted and source-currentness plus FORGE path/conflict validation remain the safety boundary. - FORGE/SRE sidecar source-authority cut, July 5: `campaign-sidecar-scheduler.mjs` no longer synthesizes a sidecar source Frame or CORE transaction from `turnContext` when CORE-first runtime ledger projections cannot find the source ingress. This keeps background work bound to CORE/SRE evidence instead of old turn-context compatibility data. - LENS runtime cache-authority cut, July 5: runtime prompt synchronization no longer builds LENS cache identity from old `runtimeTracking.revision`, old `runtimeTracking.mechanicsRevision`, or old runtime journal rows. `coreProjectionFreshnessEvidence(...)` now preserves compact CORE `revisions`, and `lensCampaignContextForPromptSync(...)` uses CORE-owned mechanics/runtime revision evidence only as context while removing self-invalidating LENS prompt/runtime revision counters from the cache-key domain vector. A chat-native regression first proved the bug red by mutating only old `runtimeTracking` revision/journal rows with raw canaries and observing `installed` instead of `reused`; after the cut, unchanged prompt context reuses the LENS cache and does not reinstall Directive prompt blocks. - Scene Handshake provider-failure fallback removal, July 5: the legacy `runSceneHandshakeSettlement(...)` path no longer turns a provider throw or empty provider response into deterministic `autoCommit` authority. The `providerFailureFallbackSettlement(...)` / `deterministicReplyAcceptsExplicitOrders(...)` fallback path was removed, and provider throws now produce deferred SRE Scene Handshake evidence with reason `source-settlement-provider-threw`, no `providerFailureFallback`, no prompt dirtying, no mission/scene mutation, and no settled row. This prevents provider failure from becoming source-settlement authority outside the latest-pair SRE/REPAIR owner path. - HostMessageId ambiguity authority cut, July 5: CORE/runtime response and ingress lookup no longer treats SillyTavern `hostMessageId` as stable authority when that host id is reused. `findLedgerIngress(...)`, `findLedgerIngressAsync(...)`, `findLedgerResponse(...)`, and `findLedgerResponseAsync(...)` now return a row for host-message lookup only when exactly one CORE projection row matches; duplicate host ids fail closed to `null`, while stable CORE ids and transaction ids continue to resolve. `updateDirectiveResponse(...)` now prefers CORE `id`/`responseId`, allows host-message matching only when the caller explicitly opts in, and refuses ambiguous host-id updates instead of mutating the first or latest positional row. Stale tests were converted to CORE/v2 assertions: duplicate host ids must fail closed, and helper assertions read the current `responses` projection alias instead of old runtime ledger rows. - Latest-pair SRE revision-authority cut, July 5: latest-pair Scene Handshake settlement no longer uses old `campaignState.runtimeTracking.revision` or `campaignState.runtimeTracking.mechanicsRevision` as pre-apply authority. `settleLatestPairSceneHandshakeSource(...)` now passes no `baseRevision` from old runtime counters into `stateDeltaGateway.applyOperations(...)`, records expected revisions as `null`, and keeps only the post-apply `appliedRevision` / `appliedMechanicsRevision` returned by the gateway as result telemetry. `buildLatestPairSceneSnapshot(...)` and the legacy Scene Handshake snapshot helper now emit `runtimeRevision: null` and `mechanicsRevision: null`, and ledger records preserve `runtimeRevisionBefore: null` instead of converting absent authority into stale revision `0`. This keeps SRE source-currentness and CORE apply results as authority while preventing old runtime revision counters from becoming a hidden latest-pair settlement gate. The regression was proven red in `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs` before the patch: the latest-pair owner still read `campaignState.runtimeTracking?.revision` / `mechanicsRevision` and passed `baseRevision` from old runtime state. - FORGE same-worker append conflict cut, July 5: FORGE path-conflict detection now distinguishes overwrite-style conflicts from ordered append batches. `findForgePathConflict(...)` still rejects repeated non-append writes to the same path and cross-worker collisions, but it allows a single worker to emit multiple ordered `append` operations to the same array path, such as a relationship sidecar adding multiple `relationships.memoryLedger` records. The live failure evidence came from `directive-soak-b` CORE diagnostics for `save-1783283947522-3-e7981307`: `core-diagnostic-000036:forge` rejected the accepted sidecar batch with `reason: "path-conflict"` on `relationships.memoryLedger`, `firstWorkerId: "relationship"`, and `secondWorkerId: "relationship"`, causing continuity, relationship, and Command Bearing sidecars to fail closed before old mutation while crew/ship no-changed. The regression was proven red in `test-campaign-sidecar-scheduler.mjs` before the patch by using a real CORE Store v2 background settlement path and a relationship packet with two `append` operations to `relationships.memoryLedger`; the scheduler returned `['rejected', 'rejected']` instead of `['applied', 'applied']`. - CORE/v2 revision-authority cut for resume, FORGE, and LENS evidence, July 5: state-delta application, sidecar scheduling, active-save resume cursors, runtime terminal-state preservation, context plans, player-safe prompt sources, continuity source Frames, continuity projection matrices, and projection-hint/fact-use aging now prefer `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.revisions` when `runtimeAuthority: "coreStoreV2"` is present. - REPAIR rollback old-snapshot authority cut, July 5: committed-outcome delete rollback authorization no longer falls back to `ledgerEntry.snapshotBefore.runtimeTracking.revision`, and runtime-app no longer passes a synthetic `snapshotBefore.runtimeTracking.revision` object into REPAIR authorization. REPAIR now accepts restore revision only from REPAIR projection/source-mutation evidence that was derived from the CORE checkpoint path. The same slice tightened `repair-command-boundary.mjs` checkpoint rollback restore: it still persists compact CORE read projections when available, but it never repopulates restored `runtimeTracking.ingressLedger`, `runtimeTracking.responseLedger`, or `runtimeTracking.recoveryJournal` from the merged current ledger view or current old runtime ledgers. Regression proof deliberately gives REPAIR a stale legacy retained snapshot revision `42` with no REPAIR projection and requires rollback authorization to stay blocked; a REPAIR/CORE projection restore revision `7` remains authorized. Rollback restore proof gives the current state legacy ingress/response/recovery rows and a checkpoint containing old ledger rows, then requires the applied restored state to keep all three runtime ledger arrays empty. - CORE live-store row-membership authority cut, July 5: `runtime-ledger-view.mjs` no longer lets state-carried `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections` become runtime authority when a live `coreTurnStore.readProjections()` path is present. A live CORE store now owns row membership: state-carried projection rows may only decorate matching live CORE rows, while state-only ingress/response/recovery rows are blocked; if the live store is empty, unavailable, or async from a sync caller, the sync view fails closed instead of borrowing stale state authority. Loaded/offline v2 state projections remain readable only when no live CORE store is provided. Async Outcome Integrity edit/correction paths were moved to async CORE reads instead of relying on stale state projections, and ResponseDispatcher diagnostic-only paths now leave response projection rows unminted unless a real CORE response projection exists. Stale compatibility tests were converted to CORE/v2 assertions: fake CORE stores now expose ingress/response/recovery projections explicitly, diagnostic failures stay diagnostic-only, stalled CORE reads no longer authorize host delegation from state fallback, and state-only old rows no longer satisfy live runtime authority. - CORE/REPAIR Outcome Integrity async authority cut, July 5: Correct-as-Swipe candidate and lifecycle updates no longer call sync `createRuntimeLedgerView(...)` or sync Outcome Integrity response reads with the live async CORE store. - REPAIR compact checkpoint-ref authorization hardening, July 5: rollback authorization no longer treats `preOutcomeRevision`, `restoreRevision`, `shouldRestoreRevision`, retained snapshots, or loaded restore state as sufficient authority. `recordSourceMutationRecovery(...)` now records rollback-ready recovery only when caller evidence includes a compact CORE checkpoint ref; `buildRollbackActuationDecision(...)` and committed-outcome delete rollback require `coreCheckpointRefFromEvidence(...)` plus `hasCoreCheckpointAuthority(...)`; and `executeRollbackActuation(...)` blocks before recording rollback actuation unless a compact checkpoint ref is present. - CORE terminal pending-decision authority cut, July 5: terminal outcome decisions no longer use `runtimeTracking.endConditionLedger` or ledger-derived terminal interactions as active pending authority. `campaign-end-condition-service.mjs` now requires `coreTurnStore.recordPendingInteraction(...)` for terminal checkpoint creation, requires `coreTurnStore.resolvePendingInteraction(...)` for replay / Push On / keep-ending resolution, and treats `endConditionLedger` as a `coreStoreV2` projection used for checkpoint text and UI detail. `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs` no longer falls back from active pending interactions or active terminal ids to `ledgerTerminalInteraction(...)`; it hydrates CORE pending projections before classification and after terminal mechanics commits so terminal checkpoint posting and reply routing remain CORE-owned. That hydration is timeout-bounded through the existing CORE projection read budget, so stalled CORE reads do not block host-native continuation and do not authorize pending resolution from old state fallback. Stale compatibility tests were converted to CORE/v2 assertions: terminal service tests require `corePendingInteractionProjection` records, terminal resolution fixtures must resolve CORE pending rows, ledger-only terminal reply fixtures are now CORE-backed, and skeleton proof rejects ledger fallback authority while still allowing terminal ledger projection display helpers. - CORE/v2 freshness old-counter poison cut, July 5: freshness arbitration is now locked to the frozen exit-list rule that old `runtimeTracking.revision` and `runtimeTracking.mechanicsRevision` are not authority whenever either side has CORE/v2 runtime authority. The production comparator already gated old revision/mechanics fallback behind `!hasCoreV2RuntimeAuthority`; stale compatibility coverage was converted into explicit CORE/v2 assertions so an old in-memory revision spike cannot outrank a same-chat CORE authoritative candidate, and the skeleton guard now fails if the old counters move outside the CORE/v2 authority gate. - LENS prompt-revision ownership cut, July 5: prompt revision authority moved out of old `runtimeTracking.promptContext` and into compact LENS-owned `directive.lensPromptRevisionRecord.v1` evidence under `directiveRuntimeEvidence.lensPromptRevisionRecord`. `lens-prompt-scheduler.mjs` now emits the record for installed prompt packets, `recordPromptContextRevision(...)` and `recordContextPlan(...)` project the compact LENS record instead of persisting prompt block metadata under runtime tracking, sidecar prompt-only state merge carries the LENS record instead of copying `prompt.runtimeTracking.promptContext`, runtime freshness merge preserves the LENS record with prompt revision/hash locator fields, and activation failure cleanup no longer mutates old prompt-context status. The record stores only hashes/counts/refs for prompt keys, external prompt environment, and prompt budget trace; raw prompt text, raw external context, and block bodies are not stored. Stale compatibility tests were converted to LENS assertions: prompt context rebuild still advances from binding/runtimeResume locator revisions, runtime persist preserves the LENS record without reviving old promptContext, LENS flush exposes the compact revision record with external-context refs, and the skeleton guard rejects production writes/copies of `runtimeTracking.promptContext` as authority. The proof-harness fix keeps the model-review request's contradiction watchlist broad enough to include the saucer-underside canary, tells the reviewer not to treat explicitly negated watchlist phrases as contradictions, and suppresses only model-assisted contradiction findings where the evidence quote negates the matched watchlist term and deterministic checks saw the fact without contradiction. - CORE terminal decision ledger authority cut, July 5/6: the terminal decision ledger is now CORE/v2 projection authority instead of old `runtimeTracking.endConditionLedger` authority. `terminal-decision-ledger-view.mjs` reads and writes `directiveRuntimeEvidence.coreStoreReadProjections.terminalDecisionLedger`, clears the retired old root, and normalizes only CORE-owned terminal projection rows. `core-store-v2.mjs` records `terminalDecisionLedgerProjected` events and exposes `readProjections().terminalDecisionLedger`; `active-save-facade-v2.mjs` persists/reloads the projection as `runtimeTerminalDecisionLedgerProjected`; `runtime-ledger-view.mjs` merges terminal decisions with other CORE read projections; `state-delta-gateway.mjs`, `repair-command-boundary.mjs`, and `runtime-app.mjs` no longer preserve or restore terminal decision authority from the old root. `campaign-end-condition-service.mjs` now records CORE terminal decision snapshots for detection/checkpoint posting, replay, Push On, keep-ending, and branch/save resolution, failing closed if CORE projection recording is missing. `campaign/end-conditions.mjs` returns Push On continuation evidence through the CORE terminal projection, and stale compatibility tests were converted to assert the CORE view while proving the retired root stays empty or ignored. This closes the explorer-identified terminal decision red for exits 1 and 2 at deterministic depth; live certification remains Wave 4 proof of the finished path, not new redesign scope.
- Operational report classifier cut, July 5: quoted bridge report requests such as "Nayar, give me the clean operational picture..." and bounded status-summary requests now route through deterministic `consequentialCommand` / `directivePosted` rather than allowing the Utility provider to downgrade them into host continuation. This prevents shallow ST host replies like literal `...` from satisfying CORE host-native proof while failing story quality. The fast path is deliberately narrow to operational report/status requests, so active hail commands still allow provider review. Focused proof passed for `test-turn-intent-classifier-fixtures.mjs`; bounded live proof shows the first two Ashes report turns are now Directive-owned `committedOutcome` rows with substantive bridge reports.
- Live Playwright blocker from the async chat-turn cut, July 3: one-user `directive-soak-b` Playwright runs repeatedly timed out after the player send while waiting for browser evaluation to settle. Evidence artifacts: `artifacts/live-smoke/async-core-chat-turn-single-user-2026-07-03`, `artifacts/live-smoke/async-core-chat-turn-single-user-retry-2026-07-03`, `artifacts/live-smoke/async-core-chat-turn-timeout-guard-single-user-2026-07-03`, and `artifacts/live-smoke/async-core-chat-turn-provider-gated-single-user-2026-07-03`. The live sidecars show CORE `turnObserved` plus SRE `sourceSettlement` preflight, then no model-call/response/recovery projection before the harness loses browser eval; the save index later records a recoverable chat-turn processing failure summary. The first mitigation added an explicit `sourceSettlementLatestPair` timeout, but generation-router already had timeout support and the live page remained busy; the second mitigation gated the latest-pair provider behind explicit `enableDefaultLatestPairSettlementProvider`/`runLatestPairSettlementProvider` opt-in so normal live turns no longer auto-started that optional provider. That provider gate is superseded by the native latest-pair scene-adapter cut and the July 5 Scene Handshake latest-pair live revalidation above; keeping it would block SRE owner certification. Treat the July 3 timeout artifacts as regression evidence for harness/browser-freeze behavior and provider-failure classification, not as a current Scene Handshake owner-root blocker.
- CORE response-retry closeout, July 2: `run-alpha-gate.mjs` initially failed at `test-chat-response-recovery.mjs` after the CORE-first recovery cut because the old fixture used a legacy `runtimeTracking.recoveryJournal` row with no CORE transaction or `allowedActions`, and the current REPAIR authorizer correctly refused non-CORE response retry actuation. The focused test now uses a real CORE v2 transaction and `responseRetryRequired` recovery case instead of legacy recovery authority. The runtime fix is in `chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`: CORE response-retry projection lookup no longer uses the placeholder `responseRetryRequired` projection id as the retry post idempotency key, and later resolved CORE recovery rows suppress earlier required rows with the same recovery id so duplicate retry calls do not reopen a closed case. Focused proof passed for `test-chat-response-recovery.mjs`, `test-chat-turn-orchestrator.mjs`, `test-response-dispatcher-core-bridge.mjs`, and `test-runtime-ledger-view.mjs`; the full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` then passed `201` checks, including both 5000-message scale gates and the external-context/five-user certification preflight tests.
- Structured Recall retrieval metadata slice, July 2: `recall-index.mjs` now normalizes first-class retrieval metadata on Recall Index entries: retrieval mode, bounded priority, audience, known-by actors, source authority, and sanitized RAG hints. `createRecallQuery(...)` accepts retrieval-mode, audience, known-by, and source-authority facets, and `queryRecallIndex(...)` filters mismatches before scoring while still keeping optional semantic/vector candidates non-authoritative and disabled unless explicitly requested. `recall-index.schema.json` now declares the retrieval metadata object and query facets. Focused proof in `test-directive-recall-index-contracts.mjs` covers package/reviewed-import-style facets, deterministic package-lore filtering, known-by/audience/source-authority scoring, semantic candidates remaining diagnostic-only, and raw prompt/vector/embedding/secret canary stripping from RAG hints and result refs. Adjacent proof passed for `test-architecture-redesign-scale-harness.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-lens-prompt-budget-lane-contracts.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). Remaining Recall work is still auxiliary recall artifact storage/fork rewriting; this slice closes the explicit structured lore/RAG metadata contract gap without adding a required vector database or importing ST Lorebooks as Directive authority.
- Director package retrieval-to-Recall bridge, July 2: `dataset-index.mjs` now builds package-authority Recall Index entries from crew and ship director cards, carrying hash-only card refs, card/source facets, lanes, keywords, bounded package previews, retrieval mode, priority, audience, known-by actors, source authority, and sanitized RAG hints. `runDirectorRetrieval(...)` now exposes Recall Index diagnostics and packet-level `recallRefs` for every audience while preserving the existing Director packet selection path; this lets LENS/SRE/REPAIR/FORGE consume structured package lore metadata without treating ST Lorebooks, raw Director card payloads, or vector stores as Directive authority. `director-card.schema.json` now allows authored retrieval facets (`mode`, `audience(s)`, `knownBy`, `sourceAuthority`, `ragHints`) for future packages. Focused proof in `test-director-retrieval-orchestration.mjs` verifies Ashes shuttle package Recall refs for Intrepid shuttle approach/shuttlebay facts, package source authority, narrator audience filtering, retrieval-mode scoring, packet exposure, and raw package constraint/anchor exclusion from Recall diagnostics. Adjacent proof passed for `test-crew-retrieval-fixture.mjs`, `test-directive-recall-index-contracts.mjs`, `test-campaign-package-importer.mjs`, `validate-campaign-package.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-scale-harness.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-runtime-host-injection.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). Remaining Recall work is auxiliary artifact storage/fork rewriting; structured package/dataset loader/query gating is now implemented at deterministic depth.
- Recall auxiliary artifact storage/fork cut, July 2: CORE background batches now persist accepted worker `recallEntries` into bounded `directive.recallIndexSegment.v1` auxiliary artifacts under the CORE save path, while the event bundle stores only compact `recallAuxiliaryRefs` rather than full entry bodies. Auxiliary entries are normalized through the Recall Index contract before write, stripping raw transcripts, selected text, prompts, provider output, vector payloads, embeddings, secrets, and Qdrant payloads. `copyCoreStoreStateV2ForSaveBranch(...)` now reads source Recall auxiliary refs, applies the deterministic Save As `directive.recallSourceMutation.v1`, writes forked target-save/target-branch Recall auxiliary segments, preserves `forkedFromRef` provenance, and rewrites copied CORE background events to point at the target refs. Runtime Save Game As now exposes `coreBranchClone.recallAuxiliaryRewrite` alongside the existing compact mutation evidence, so production no longer reports Recall fork intent without doing the auxiliary rewrite. Focused proof in `test-core-store-v2.mjs` covers source auxiliary segment persistence, raw canary stripping, target Save As fork segment writes, target-only event refs, target save/branch scoping, and preserved source provenance. Adjacent proof passed for `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-directive-recall-index-contracts.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). Remaining Recall work is to broaden producer coverage for all FORGE/REPAIR paths that should supply full Recall entries, but the storage/fork substrate is now real rather than evidence-only.
- FORGE Recall auxiliary producer cut, July 2: `createForgeBatchCommit(...)` now carries worker-produced `recallEntries` as write-only CORE input, and the FORGE coordinator passes those entries into every accepted background batch path that already commits `recallEntryRefs`. CORE still strips full entries out of the persisted event bundle and writes only compact `recallAuxiliaryRefs`, so scene/phase seal production now generates real auxiliary Recall segments rather than only refs. `test-forge-scene-phase-seal.mjs` now proves `settleScenePhaseSeal(...)` carries one Recall entry, hydrated CORE projections expose one auxiliary ref, the auxiliary segment can be read back, and raw provider summary/transcript canaries do not survive in refs or stored entries. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-forge-internal-background-settlement.mjs`, `test-background-projection-batch.mjs`, `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-runtime-stage18-rerun-branch-recovery.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). Remaining Recall producer work is narrower: REPAIR/source-mutation and any non-scene FORGE workers that create first-class Recall entries still need explicit producer coverage.
- REPAIR source-mutation Recall rewrite cut, July 2: `markRecoveryRequired(...)` now detects compact `sourceMutation.recallSourceMutation`, reads the current CORE Recall auxiliary snapshot, applies `applyRecallSourceMutation(...)`, writes a replacement `directive.recallIndexSegment.v1` snapshot, and stores compact `recallAuxiliaryRewrite`/`recallAuxiliaryRefs` evidence on the CORE recovery projection. The auxiliary ref collector now treats source-mutation rewrites as snapshot replacement rather than append-only history, so later Save As forks only current valid Recall entries and does not revive stale source-edit/delete/selected-swipe entries from older auxiliary segments. Focused proof in `test-core-store-v2.mjs` covers accepted Recall entries becoming stale after a source edit, raw replacement-text exclusion, projection-level rewrite refs, and a later Save As producing zero forked refs from stale source entries. Runtime proof in `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs` verifies real source-edit recovery projections now carry `directive.recallAuxiliaryRewrite.v1` evidence. Adjacent proof passed for `test-message-recovery.mjs`, `test-repair-runtime.mjs`, `test-architecture-redesign-system-skeletons.mjs`, `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`, `test-forge-scene-phase-seal.mjs`, and full `run-alpha-gate.mjs` (`201` checks). Remaining Recall producer work is now limited to non-scene FORGE workers that create first-class Recall entries.
- FORGE pressure/arc Recall auxiliary proof cut, July 2: `createPressureArcDigestWorkerResult(...)` already produced first-class Recall entries, and the shared `createForgeBatchCommit(...)`/FORGE coordinator wiring now passes those entries into CORE just like scene/phase seals. The focused gap was proof: `test-forge-pressure-arc-digest.mjs` now verifies accepted pressure/arc batches carry one `recallEntries` item, hydrated CORE projections expose one `directive.recallIndexSegment.v1` auxiliary ref, the auxiliary segment can be read back, and raw pressure/arc provider-summary/body/thread/callback canaries do not survive in refs or stored entries. Adjacent proof passed for `test-core-store-v2.mjs`, `test-forge-scene-phase-seal.mjs`, `test-forge-internal-background-settlement.mjs`, and `test-background-projection-batch.mjs`. This closed the known non-scene FORGE Recall producer/storage proof gap.
- LENS hybrid Recall consumption cut, July 2: `runDirectorRetrieval(...)` now accepts CORE Recall entries from auxiliary storage/projections and merges them with package-derived Recall entries before querying. Retrieval queries use deterministic, scene-seal, reviewed-import, and package modes together, so CORE/FORGE scene-seal evidence and package lore can appear in the same audience packet without a vector dependency or external-tool authority. `lens-prompt-scheduler.mjs` now turns packet-level `recallRefs` and `omittedRecallRefs` into the Recall prompt budget lane automatically when explicit lanes are not provided, and packet refresh keeps only compact ref fields so raw source text, raw seal summaries, package previews, and stale Recall text do not survive in installed packets or budget traces. Focused proof in `test-director-retrieval-orchestration.mjs` verifies a CORE scene-seal Recall ref outranks package refs for the Ashes shuttle rendezvous while still retaining package shuttlebay Recall; `test-lens-prompt-budget-lane-contracts.mjs` verifies automatic Recall lane inclusion, budget overflow omission, stale-source omission, and raw canary stripping. Adjacent proof passed for `test-storage-scale-5000.mjs`. Remaining Recall work was production/runtime pass-through and live/integration certification.
- Production CORE-Recall to Director/LENS cut, July 2: runtime prompt synchronization now reads current CORE Recall auxiliary entries through `runtimeCoreTurnStore.readRecallIndexAuxiliaryEntries(...)` and `readCoreRecallIndexAuxiliaryEntries(...)`, passes compact CORE Recall evidence into `createLensPromptInput(...)`, and lets `buildLensPromptPacket(...)` convert CORE entries into hash-only `recallRefs`. Runtime Director turns, provisional turns, rerun previews, the open-world coordinator, and tactical Mission Director turns now accept the same `coreRecallEntries` lane so production Director packets can query CORE scene-seal/Recall evidence alongside package Recall. Chat-native prompt sync also carries committed narrator `recallRefs` from the turn packet into the LENS prompt frame, so immediate post-turn prompt rebuilds have Recall lane evidence without re-reading raw transcript history. Focused proof passed in `test-runtime-director-turn.mjs` for runtime Director CORE Recall pass-through and raw canary stripping; `test-lens-prompt-budget-lane-contracts.mjs` for production LENS packet conversion; `test-chat-native-runtime-flow.mjs`, `test-runtime-host-injection.mjs`, `test-prompt-dirty-domains.mjs`, `test-open-world-director-coordinator-contracts.mjs`, and `test-mission-director-loop.mjs` for adjacent runtime/prompt/coordinator compatibility. Remaining Recall work is live certification: run the Ashes live lanes and prove persisted artifacts include Recall/budget evidence without full-history rewrites.

Coordinator artifact contract:

```text
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/report.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/summary.md
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/live-log.jsonl
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/readiness/<runId>-readiness/report.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/readiness/<runId>-readiness/host-extensions/compatibility.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/readiness/<runId>-readiness/host-extensions/external-context-probe.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/readiness/<runId>-readiness/host-extensions/external-context-probe.<handle>.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/report.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/summary.md
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/live-log.jsonl
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/host-extensions/external-context-summary.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/fact-checks/canary-index.json
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/transcript/readable-chat.md
artifacts/live-soak/continuity-projection-matrix-five-user/<runId>/lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/prompt-inspection/*.json
```

Readiness external-context evidence appears in:

- coordinator report: `.readiness.externalContextProbe`;
- coordinator check: `.checks[] | select(.id=="external-context-readiness-proof")`;
- readiness report: `.externalContextProbe`;
- raw probe: `readiness/<runId>-readiness/host-extensions/external-context-probe.json`.

Generation external-context evidence appears in:

- coordinator lane records: `.lanes[].externalContextProof` for the latest prompt snapshot summary and `.lanes[].externalContextGenerationProof` for pre-generation snapshot depth and rich-fixture pressure;
- coordinator check details: `.checks[] | select(.id=="external-context-generation-proof").details.lanes`;
- lane host-extension summary: `lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/host-extensions/external-context-summary.json`;
- lane prompt snapshots: `lanes/<laneId>/<runId>-<laneId>/prompt-inspection/*.json`.

For certification release proof, `.lanes[].externalContextGenerationProof.expectedScriptMessageIds` must match the 25-turn Ashes certification scope, and `.missingScriptMessageIds`, `.unexpectedScriptMessageIds`, `.duplicateScriptMessageIds`, and `.missingScriptMessageIdCount` must all be empty/zero. For rich prepared fixture users, `.lanes[].externalContextGenerationProof.richFixturePressure.status` must be `pass`. The details should show compact target evidence for ST Lorebooks/World Info, Memory Books, Summaryception, VectFox, `finalHostPromptMayIncludeExternal`, and redaction reasons without raw prompt bodies or external generated content.

The lane `host-extensions/external-context-summary.json` artifact is intentionally not a raw prompt-inspection archive. It must pass the same unsafe-field scan used by coordinator and full-certification preflight: redaction reasons and redaction path metadata are allowed, but raw lorebook text, generated Memory Books text, Summaryception summaries, VectFox payloads, embeddings, endpoint URLs, collection names, API keys, secrets, provider bodies, and hidden Director material fail the lane. It must also prove final-host-prompt external influence was possible and provide non-placeholder compact evidence for ST Lorebooks/World Info, Memory Books, Summaryception, and VectFox.

Host-native completion evidence appears in:

- smoke report: `.browser.chatCampaignFlow.rounds[].hostNativeCompletion.persisted`, `.browser.chatCampaignFlow.rounds[].hostNativeCompletionRequirement`, and `.browser.chatCampaignFlow.hostNativeCompletionProof`;
- compact smoke summary: `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionStatus`, `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionProofSource`, `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionProofCompletionSource`, `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionCount`, `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionFailureCount`, `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionRequiredCount`, `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionRequiredPassCount`, `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionRequiredFailureCount`, and `.chatCampaign.hostNativeCompletionMaxLatencyMs`;
- delegated soak check: `.checks[] | select(.id=="live-host-native-completion-proof")`;
- coordinator lane records: `.lanes[].hostNativeCompletionProof`;
- coordinator aggregate check: `.checks[] | select(.id=="host-native-completion-core-proof")`.

Certification requires `source: "coreStoreResponseLedger"`, `completionSource: "coreProjection"`, `completedHostContinueCount > 0`, no failed hostContinue completions, no raw assistant text in proof artifacts, and a passing required-turn binding for every scripted `hostNativeCompletionRequired` message. For Ashes, the required object is currently `scriptMessageId: "soak-turn-03"`, `turn: 3`, `expectedRoute: "hostContinue"`, and `expectedResponseStrategy: "injectAndContinue"`. Count-only proof is limited evidence and must not satisfy release certification.

Model-call failure policy evidence appears in:

- lane report policy: `.modelCallPolicy.failurePolicyEvidence`;
- lane check: `.checks[] | select(.id=="live-model-call-failure-policy")`;
- coordinator lane records: `.lanes[].modelCallFailurePolicy`;
- coordinator aggregate check: `.checks[] | select(.id=="model-call-failure-policy")`;
- full-certification preflight lane records: `.lanes[].modelCallPolicy`;
- full-certification preflight aggregate check: `.checks[] | select(.id=="model-call-failure-policy")`.

Certification requires lane-owned durable evidence first. Raw `smoke-chat-soak/report.json` retained model calls may explain or populate the policy object, but they are not a substitute for `modelCallPolicy.failurePolicyEvidence` in the lane report.

Message-mutation actuation evidence appears in the Architecture Redesign release bundle as `message-mutation-actuation-proof.json`:

- top-level `kind: "directive.sillytavernMessageMutation.actuationProof.v1"`;
- top-level `driver: "playwright"`, non-human `sillyTavernUser`, `defaultUserTouched: false`, and served-extension freshness evidence;
- `artifacts` refs for the source-edit, source-delete, assistant-edit, assistant-delete, and selected-swipe child reports;
- five passing scenarios with ids `source-edit`, `source-delete`, `assistant-edit`, `assistant-delete`, and `selected-swipe`.

The proof producer must validate more than those top-level fields. Edit/delete child reports must include `directive.sourceMutationProof.v1`: target host id, source role, tracked ingress/response kind, mutation kind, original/replacement text hashes or delete original hash, native host-control evidence, ledger status after mutation, CORE recovery status/case refs, compact REPAIR decision kind/action/event type, and prompt-context delta. Old recovery-journal/count deltas are optional legacy telemetry only and cannot be the owner evidence. Selected-swipe child reports must include `directive.sourceIntegrityProof.v1`: selected host assistant source id, `actuationMode: "native-host-swipe-control"`, `nativeHostControlMoved: true`, selected swipe index, swipe count, source-integrity status, selected-hash equality with the accepted assistant variant, absence of discarded-swipe canaries from committed state, SRE/Scene Handshake decision status, and source hash refs. Setup-assisted native selected-swipe actuation is proven at `artifacts/live-smoke/selected-swipe-actuation/2026-07-02T23-41-07-914Z/selected-swipe-actuation-report.json`. Final natural latest-response proof is now also proven at `artifacts/live-smoke/selected-swipe-actuation/2026-07-03T00-11-15-605Z/selected-swipe-actuation-report.json`: `targetSetup: null`, non-default `directive-soak-b`, served extension fresh, latest CORE-recorded Ashes host-generation row `51`, native `.last_mes .swipe_right` actuation, selected index `0 -> 1`, `nativeHostControlMoved: true`, `sourceIntegrity: "clean"`, and no diagnostic fallback. The code implication is part of the plan: Correct-as-Swipe and the SillyTavern adapter must use CORE/ledger response authority for natural host-generation rows, while unrecorded assistant rows remain ineligible. Shallow scenario shells are acceptable only as release-bundle summary inputs after the strict producer has already validated the child reports, and the bundle preflight now rejects count-only mutation summaries that omit CORE/REPAIR owner proof or use staged selected-swipe evidence as release actuation.

July 3 live mutation proof added a stricter REPAIR/SRE invariant: SillyTavern `mesid` is positional and can be reused after delete/reindex, so no CORE/legacy merge, native host event, or proof selector may treat `hostMessageId` alone as durable identity. Stable `ingressId`/`responseId` wins when present; host-only native events may consult the explicit hot runtime overlay through `createRuntimeLedgerView(..., { runtimeOverlay: true })`, but proof selectors must still prefer stable ids/transaction aliases and must not treat raw runtimeTracking host id rows as authority ahead of CORE projections. This was proven by a failed assistant-delete artifact that invalidated the correct response while attaching a stale CORE recovery transaction, then fixed and re-proven on Ashes save `(73)` row `5` with matching response/recovery transaction `...:4:1e6676ac`. The strict five-scenario actuation proof now passes with no warnings at `artifacts/live-soak/sillytavern-campaign/message-mutation-actuation/message-mutation-actuation-2026-07-03T03-32-13-117Z/message-mutation-actuation-proof.json`.

The readiness probe must classify `stLorebooks`, `memoryBooks`, `summaryception`, and `vectFox` as `browser-confirmed`, `disabled`, `not-installed`, `disk-confirmed`, `settings-only`, `unavailable`, or `indeterminate`. `browser-confirmed`, `disabled`, and `not-installed` are pass statuses. `disk-confirmed` or `settings-only` without browser confirmation is limited evidence and should remain warning/failure depending on the run requirement.

Readiness runs that request external context compatibility and write artifacts must also write `host-extensions/external-context-summary.json`, and the report must expose `externalContextSummary`. The summary is required even before full Ashes certification because it is the compact, redacted bridge between live browser/runtime observation and release preflight. Current five-user proof: `artifacts/live-soak/sillytavern-campaign/external-context-five-user-live-2026-07-03Tsummary/host-extensions/external-context-summary.json` validates as pass with five captures, five target summaries, all required targets useful, no placeholder summaries, no forbidden fields, no missing fields, and only redaction metadata for `raw-payload` and `secret`.

Exit gate:

- 5000-message synthetic campaign passes size/write/load/replay targets.
- HostContinue and directiveCommit generation-start targets pass under controlled provider latency.
- Live proof records persisted transaction timing, not only chat output.
- Live proof records terminal host-native completion from CORE response projections, not only release timing.
- Live proof records external context-extension state without treating external context as Directive authority.

## Stage 14: Documentation And Cleanup

Goal: make the new architecture discoverable and remove stale instructions.

Owner: Agent-0, optional docs worker.

Tasks:

- Update [Documentation Index](../DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md).
- Update or supersede [State Transactions And Recovery](../technical/STATE_TRANSACTIONS_AND_RECOVERY.md).
- Update [Player Turn Sequence](../technical/PLAYER_TURN_SEQUENCE.md).
- Update [Testing Strategy](../testing/TESTING_STRATEGY.md) with new v2 tests and live proof.
- Promote the external context-extension compatibility boundary from this plan into durable manuals after runtime behavior proves it. The note must cover ST-Lorebooks / ST Lorebooks / World Info, Memory Books, Summaryception, and VectFox, and say Directive is compatible by preserving and observing host surfaces; it does not operate those tools, call their internals, auto-import their generated material, or treat them as Directive authority.
- Update operator-facing language only after runtime behavior exists.
- Mark old Scene Handshake/Reconciliation/sidecar docs with the new SRE/FORGE framing.
- Remove obsolete implementation-plan caveats once cutover is complete.

Exit gate:

- Docs index points to proposal and implementation plan.
- Technical docs reflect current v2 behavior.
- No stale doc says hot-path runtime writes full campaign saves.

## Agent Prompt Templates

### Contracts Worker

```text
You are the Contracts worker for Directive Architecture Redesign. Read docs/planning/ARCHITECTURE_REDESIGN_PROPOSAL.md and docs/planning/ARCHITECTURE_REDESIGN_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Your lane is schemas, metrics, and no-behavior-change tests only. Do not edit runtime hot paths. Own only the files Agent-0 lists. Handoff with schemas added, tests added, commands run, and any contract questions.
```

### CORE Storage Worker

```text
You are the CORE Storage worker. Your lane is v2 storage substrate, segment APIs, manifest-last commit, checkpoints, and hot-write/read scale proof. Do not edit chat-turn-orchestrator, runtime-app, state-delta-gateway, or transaction-state unless Agent-0 explicitly assigns them. Handoff with changed files, storage invariants, tests run, and authority-cutover risks.
```

### CORE Runtime Worker

```text
You are the CORE Runtime worker. Your lane is Frame creation, fast gate, visible response timing, and mechanics/narration split for the specific slice Agent-0 assigns. Do not touch SRE, REPAIR, FORGE, or LENS internals unless assigned. Handoff with route timing evidence, exactly-one-response evidence, tests run, and shared-file integration needs.
```

### REPAIR/SRE Worker

```text
You are the REPAIR/SRE worker. Your lane is latest-boundary recovery and source-integrity settlement. REPAIR owns edit/delete/retry/rerun decisions. SRE owns source integrity and settlement modes. Do not edit prompt install, sidecar batch apply, or storage substrate. Handoff with recovery cases covered, stale-source guards, tests run, and integration requests.
```

### Recall/Budget Worker

```text
You are the Recall/Budget worker for Directive Architecture Redesign. Read the Narrative Engine Transfer Pivot in docs/planning/ARCHITECTURE_REDESIGN_PROPOSAL.md and the Narrative Engine Transfer Workstream in docs/planning/ARCHITECTURE_REDESIGN_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Your lane is Directive Recall Index contracts, deterministic retrieval facets, LENS prompt budget lanes, omission traces, and 5000-message scale impact. Narrative Engine is a design reference, not a port target. Do not add a required vector database, do not store raw transcript/prompt/provider/external content, and do not change source authority. Handoff with schemas, lane budgets, query/invalidation rules, tests, and scale risks.
```

### Witness/Correction Worker

```text
You are the Witness/Correction worker. Your lane is witness-scoped continuity facts and evidence-backed Correct-as-Swipe. SRE owns evidence verdicts and selected-source integrity. REPAIR owns correction cases and allowed actions. Host adapters append candidate swipes; selected-swipe acceptance remains the continuity boundary. Do not mutate accepted assistant prose directly, rerun mechanics, or treat external lore/memory/summary/vector hits as authority. Handoff with fact fields, anti-telepathy tests, correction-case flow, candidate swipe provenance, and integration requests.
```

### FORGE/LENS Worker

```text
You are the FORGE/LENS worker. Your lane is background batch coordination, scene/phase seal settlement, pressure/arc digest refresh, prompt dirty scheduling, prompt budget lanes, and external prompt-environment diagnostics. Keep worker prompts/schemas typed and separate. Do not change mechanics commit or REPAIR policy. Do not clear non-Directive prompt keys or import external extension memory as Directive state. Handoff with batch apply count, prompt rebuild count, scene-seal refs, prompt budget trace evidence, external prompt key coexistence evidence, cancellation behavior, tests run, and any generation-router changes needed.
```

### External Compatibility Worker

```text
You are the External Compatibility worker. Your lane is SillyTavern context-extension coexistence for native ST Lorebooks/World Info, Memory Books, Summaryception, and VectFox. Inspect installed/live surfaces without copying implementation or raw user content. Do not integrate with extension internals, call private APIs as a dependency, import generated memories/summaries/vector hits into Directive state, or change runtime authority policy. Handoff with observable prompt keys, World Info surfaces, chat metadata markers, visibility markers, settings hashes, unavailable signals, redaction risks, prompt-key preservation fixtures, and live-proof artifact expectations.
```

### QA Worker

```text
You are the QA worker. Your lane is deterministic gates, scale fixtures, recall/scene-seal/prompt-budget fixtures, external context-extension fixtures, alpha-gate additions by assignment, and live-proof planning. Do not change product behavior unless Agent-0 assigns a test-only hook or fixture helper. Handoff with exact commands, pass/fail output summaries, artifacts written, redaction checks, size budgets, and residual risks.
```

### Spec-Compliance Reviewer

```text
You are the Spec-Compliance reviewer for a Directive Architecture Redesign slice. Read the assigned slice intake, the relevant section of docs/planning/ARCHITECTURE_REDESIGN_PROPOSAL.md, the relevant section of docs/planning/ARCHITECTURE_REDESIGN_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, and the worker handoff or changed files. Do not broaden scope and do not implement fixes unless Agent-0 explicitly reassigns you as a worker. Answer whether the patch satisfies the current proposal and plan. Check owner boundaries, required contracts, selected verification, deferred gates, Narrative Engine transfer impact, external-context impact, and pre-alpha cutover rules. Handoff with SPEC_APPROVED or SPEC_REJECTED, exact missing requirements, file/line refs, and the smallest required correction.
```

### Code-Quality Reviewer

```text
You are the Code-Quality reviewer for a Directive Architecture Redesign slice. Read the assigned slice intake, worker handoff, tests, and changed files. Do not re-litigate product scope. Inspect ordering, split-brain bridges, idempotency, stale-source guards, raw-content leaks, unbounded payload growth, old-authority revival, prompt-key ownership, and maintainability. Handoff with QUALITY_APPROVED or QUALITY_REJECTED, exact findings ordered by severity, file/line refs, commands run, and any hidden risk that should become a macro blocker or deferred micro item.
```

## Integration Checklist

Before closing any stage:

- Current pass and worker-concurrency choice are recorded on the Architecture Redesign Board.
- Worker handoffs are received.
- Spec-compliance reviewer handoff is received, or Agent-0 records a waiver because the slice is docs-only, mechanical, or trivial.
- Code-quality reviewer handoff is received, or Agent-0 records a waiver because the slice is docs-only, mechanical, or trivial.
- Agent-0 inspected changed files.
- Targeted tests for the lane pass.
- Recall, prompt budget, witness-scope, scene-seal, and correction-case work either has passing targeted tests or is recorded on the deferred micro board with owner and gate.
- Known red tests/artifacts are classified as macro blockers, integration backlog, micro backlog, or out of scope.
- Remaining bridge code has an owner and removal stage.
- `git diff --check` exits cleanly, ignoring CRLF warnings only when exit code is zero.
- `node tools\scripts\verify-repo-structure.mjs` passes when docs or scaffold files changed.
- `node tools\scripts\run-alpha-gate.mjs` runs after meaningful runtime/storage integration.
- Architecture Redesign Board is updated.
- Frozen files are either released or carried forward explicitly.

Before live proof:

- Installed SillyTavern extension copy is confirmed current.
- Exactly one explicit non-human `directive-soak-*` user is selected for routine live proof unless the slice has reached final Wave 4 certification or the user explicitly re-enables broader lanes.
- Deterministic gates for the touched behavior pass.
- Live script records persisted transaction artifacts.
- Provider completion time and architecture generation-start time are reported separately.
- Recall Index, prompt budget trace, scene-seal, witness-scope, and correction-case evidence is present when the exercised scenario reaches those features.
- External context diagnostics redact secrets and raw external prompt/vector payloads.

## Final Success Criteria

The redesign implementation is complete when:

1. CORE is the only runtime durable writer for turn work.
2. Frame is the only source provenance token consumed by CPM, SRE, REPAIR, FORGE, and LENS.
3. SRE and REPAIR prevent stale-source, selected-swipe, edit/delete, and dependent-response loops.
4. FORGE applies accepted background work as one batch transaction and one prompt rebuild.
5. FORGE scene/phase seals and pressure digests settle as bounded background batches and update Recall Index/LENS dirty domains without blocking visible generation.
6. LENS coalesces prompt rebuilds, records prompt revision usage honestly, and emits prompt budget traces with included/omitted refs.
7. Recall Index retrieves CORE/Frame/package/scene-seal evidence without archiving raw transcript history or treating optional semantic/vector candidates as truth.
8. Witness-scoped facts drive active-cast/protected-continuity prompt inclusion and prevent character knowledge leakage.
9. Correct-as-Swipe appends candidate swipes with evidence provenance and does not mutate accepted continuity until selected.
10. Known external context-extension tools can coexist: ST Lorebooks, Memory Books, Summaryception, and VectFox are observed/redacted, not cleared, imported, or treated as Directive authority.
11. Open-world mechanics commit bounded events/operations instead of broad root replacement.
12. No hot-path full-save rewrite remains.
13. 5000-message synthetic scale gates pass, including recall index, scene seals, prompt budget traces, correction-case stubs, and external diagnostics.
14. HostContinue and directiveCommit generation start within the required under-60-second architecture budget.
15. Live SillyTavern proof confirms persisted timing, segment sizes, Directive context revision, recall/budget/seal evidence, external context-extension state, and stale-work cancellation.
